Events & Industry Presence· Own end-to-end planning and delivery of industry events, trade shows and roundtables· Coordinate webinars in collaboration with Sales and Product· Manage event logistics, messaging, collateral and post-event follow-up strategy· Track event ROI, pipeline contribution and post-event engagement· Identify relevant awards and speaking opportunities aligned to commercial goals4. Sales Enablement· Work closely with growth team to align campaigns to revenue priorities· Develop sales enablement materials and support outbound campaigns· Ensure structured follow-up process post-events and campaigns5. Performance & Reporting· Own monthly performance reporting and dashboard management· Track MQLs, SQLs, pipeline impact and channel ROI· Manage allocated marketing budget effectivelyAbout YouYou are a commercially minded B2B marketer who enjoys taking ownership of delivery and performance.You are confident operating independently within a scale-up environment and are comfortable executing campaigns end-to-end while collaborating closely with Sales and Product teams.You thrive in a hands-on role where consistency, organisation and measurable results matter.Essential Skills & Experience· 4–6+ years’ experience in B2B marketing, ideally within SaaS, fintech or a regulated industry· Hands-on experience managing LinkedIn Ads and B2B PPC campaigns· Strong understanding of demand generation and marketing-to-sales funnel alignment· Experience owning campaigns from planning through to reporting and optimisation· Confident analysing performance data and reporting on pipeline contribution· Highly organised with the ability to manage multiple workstreams independently· Comfortable working as the sole internal marketing resourceDesirable Skills & Experience· Experience marketing to financial services organisations· Experience managing industry events, trade shows or webinars· Familiarity with longer sales cycles and account-based approaches· Experience working with external agencies or freelancersWhat we offer· Competitive salary· A dynamic and collaborative scale-up environment· The opportunity to take ownership of a well-defined marketing function· Hybrid working policy with flexibility· Regular team socials and eventsApplicationIf you are excited about the opportunity to lead and execute marketing activity within a growing B2B SaaS company, please apply via LinkedIn with your CV and Cover LetterIn your application, please include a brief example of a campaign you managed that delivered measurable commercial results.

title: Senior Marketing Manager - Performance
jd_text: SPEED & SPIRIT is what we look for in our candidates, defined by some simple values that inspire us to BE DRIVEN in our performance, BE VIBRANT in our sporting legacy, BE TOGETHER in our team spirit, and BE YOU to let our individual talent and experience shine. Applying for a job at PUMA is easy and all genders are welcome. Simply click APPLY ONLINE and follow the steps to upload your application.YOUR MISSION:WHY THIS ROLE MATTERSPUMA has never been a brand that sits still. We challenge convention, push boundaries, and celebrate the culture and energy of sport. To keep elevating our Performance categories across Oceania, we’re searching for a Senior Marketing Manager - Performance who thrives on action, loves being hands-on, and brings both confidence and resilience to every challenge.If you want a role where you truly influence brand direction and commercial outcomes, this is it.About The RoleINTRODUCTION: THE DIFFERENCE YOU’LL MAKEAs the strategic lead for Running, Teamsport, and Training, you will define, build, and execute the Performance marketing strategy for Oceania. This is a role for someone who mixes creativity with analytical thinking, can move from strategy to execution seamlessly, and understands what it takes to drive both brand desirability and commercial success.Your work will shape how athletes, consumers, and the market see PUMA in some of our most important categories.WHAT YOU WILL LEAD & WHERE YOU’LL GET YOUR HANDS DIRTYThis is not a spectator role. You’ll be the one leading, doing, deciding, executing, refining, and pushing campaigns to the finish line.Performance Strategy and PlanningLead the development and execution of the Performance category strategy across Oceania.Deliver fully integrated campaigns across digital, social, media, PR, and experiential channels.Maintain strong, consistent brand positioning across every touchpoint.Partner closely with Commercial Marketing to drive awareness, consideration, and sell‑through.Oversee end-to-end campaign planning, execution, and post-analysis.Use insights and analytics to optimise and evolve campaign performance.Performance Campaign ManagementLead full‑year marketing planning for Rebel and other key partners, plus owned retail and e‑commerce.Manage campaign execution, ensuring timelines, budgets, and deliverables are met.Maintain cohesive messaging across all platforms.Oversee post-campaign analysis and insights reporting with agency partners.Ensure all parties are aligned, briefed, and prepared ahead of launch.Stakeholder ManagementManage agency partnerships to deliver best‑in‑class output.Collaborate with Global Running and Teamsport teams for approvals, alignment, and shared strategic direction.Work closely with Sales, Merchandise, eCom, Retail, and Sports Marketing to ensure complete cohesion across campaigns and touchpoints.GTM (Go‑To‑Market) LeadershipLead the GTM process for wholesale and retail in the Performance categories.Drive impactful sell‑in presentations and support the production of campaign tools and assets.General Leadership and OperationsGuide social content planning, scheduling, and alignment with broader campaigns.Manage budgets, cost allocations, and invoice workflows.Oversee product seeding processes to support campaigns.Uphold business policies, lead learning requirements, and foster operational excellence.YOUR TALENT:Who You AreTHE MARKETER WHO WILL THRIVE HEREYou bring experience, but more importantly, you bring energy. You thrive in fast-moving environments, love creative problem solving, and never shy away from rolling up your sleeves.Your Experience5–8 years’ experience in retail, sport, lifestyle, or fashion marketing within a multi‑category brand.Tertiary qualification in Marketing or a related discipline.Proven capability in executing integrated campaigns and partnering cross‑functionally.Expertise in retail marketing, consumer behaviour, and market trends.Your StrengthsStrategic mindset backed by a hands‑on work ethic.Strong stakeholder management and communication skills.Exceptionally organised, detail‑driven, and proactive.Why Puma What Makes This a Unique OpportunityPUMA offers a culture built on speed, collaboration, and creative ambition. You’ll join a team that values innovation, encourages new ways of thinking, and gives you the autonomy to make an impact. This is an opportunity to lead Performance Marketing for one of the world’s most dynamic sports brands and to leave your mark on categories that shape how consumers experience sport in Oceania.READY TO STEP UP?If you’re a marketer who loves to build, lead, and execute with confidence, and you’re ready to shape the next chapter of PUMA Performance, we’d like to hear from you.PUMA supports over 21,000 employees across 120+ countries. The PUMA Group owns the brand PUMA, Cobra Golf and stichd, and is headquartered in Herzogenaurach, Germany.PUMA provides equal opportunities for all job applicants, regardless of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, or disability. Equality for all is one of the core principles at PUMA and we do not tolerate any form of harassment or discrimination.

title: Marketing Manager
jd_text: Job Title: Marketing Manager Location: Chotto Matte - San Francisco Salary: $65,000 - $75,000Overview We are seeking a creative, trend-driven Marketing Manager with strong skills in short-form content creation, social media management, community engagement, and paid advertising. This role is ideal for someone who thrives on social platforms, understands cultural trends, and can consistently produce engaging, high-quality content. You’ll assist in managing our social presence, build local community connections, and contribute to paid campaigns while supporting broader marketing goals. Key Responsibilities:Short-Form Content Creation Create high-quality Reels, TikToks, short videos, and organic-style content. Record and develop content inspired by current trends, audio, formats, and cultural moments. Maintain a structured and proactive content calendar. Work with the design and creative team to produce visual assets that align with brand identity. Social Media Management Manage daily operations of all major social platforms (Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, LinkedIn, etc.). Publish content across channels. Monitor platform trends and adapt content strategies to maximize reach and engagement. Engage with followers, respond to messages, and manage overall community interaction. Trend Research & Documentation Identify emerging social media trends, audio, memes, and content formats. Document trends and propose timely, brand-relevant creative concepts. Track competitor content and broader industry movements. Community Building & Local Collaborations Develop and maintain relationships with local businesses, creators, community groups, and partners. Coordinate and support local collaborations, events, and co-marketing initiatives. Identify opportunities to grow community presence both online and offline. Encourage user-generated content and foster a positive, interactive brand community. Paid Advertising Support and execute paid social campaigns (Meta, TikTok, Google Ads). Assist with audience targeting, creative testing, and performance analysis. Ensure paid and organic strategies work together cohesively. Analytics & Reporting Monitor social media insights and content performance metrics. Provide monthly reporting with recommendations for improvement. Use analytics tools such as Meta Insights, TikTok Analytics, Google Analytics, and scheduling platforms. Qualifications 2–5 years of experience in social media management, content creation, or digital marketing. Strong skills in video creation, editing, and on-camera content. Solid understanding of paid advertising across social platforms. Strong grasp of trend culture, digital storytelling, and creative strategy. Experience with editing tools (CapCut, Adobe Premiere, After Effects) and design tools (Canva, Adobe Suite). Excellent communication, organization, and multitasking abilities. Experience in community engagement or local partnerships is a plus. Key Competencies Trend awareness & creative adaptability Strong visual storytelling Social media expertise Community building & relationship development Paid advertising proficiency Data-driven strategy

title: Marketing Manager
jd_text: Salary: £35 - £38k annually dependant on experienceAnnual Leave Entitlement: 25 days (plus 10 bank holidays)Working Hours: Full-Time – 35 hours per weekContract: PermanentLocation: Ratho, Edinburgh / Hybrid-workingClosing date for applications: Friday, 13 March 2026 at 12 noonIn person interviews for this role to be confirmedPrior to any employment commencing with Scotland Food & Drink you will be asked to provide documents to demonstrate your right to work in the UK.JOB SUMMARYThe Marketing Manager is a newly created role and sits within the communications side of the Brand & Communications team, which includes the Communications Manager, the Brand & Digital Marketing Manager, the Communications & Social Media Assistant, and four events professionals. The communications and events teams work closely together and are responsible for developing and delivering Scotland Food & Drink’s brand, events and communications campaigns to support membership engagement, the food and drink sector, and the organisation’s industry profile.The role leads on marketing delivery for The Knowledge Bank, the Scotland Food & Drink Partnership’s Net Zero Commitment, the Scottish Organic Action Plan 2026 - 2029 and the industry’s delivery plan, and involves planning, creating and overseeing content across digital and brand channels to achieve campaign and organisational objectives. Working closely with the Head of Brand & Communications, the postholder leads the marketing, communications and social media strategy for these programmes, including content creation, campaign management, performance analysis, and reporting. The role also works closely with the Communications Manager and the Communications & Social Media Assistant to ensure consistency across all of Scotland Food & Drink’s delivery channels, as well as being responsible for liaising with external agencies to deliver digital campaigns and creative assets where required.The role also includes leading the strategy and development of the programme’s web pages and websites, ensuring content is maintained and aligned with organisational objectives. Close collaboration with colleagues across teams is required to coordinate communications and promotion of Scotland Food & Drink activities and initiatives.ABOUT THE ROLEYour day-to-day responsibilities may include but are not limited to:Campaign Planning & Management: Lead the planning and coordination of marketing campaigns, creating actionable briefings and ensuring alignment with organisational objectives and KPIs. Collaborate with internal departments and creative agencies to develop campaign concepts and deliver multi-channel campaigns effectively.Brand & Digital execution: Monitor execution across all aspects of the marketing mix, including digital, brand marketing and production of materials. Ensure campaigns are delivered on time, on budget and meet agreed quality standards.Analysis & Reporting: Analyse campaign performance and insights, producing clear reports for internal stakeholders. Recommend and implement continuous improvements to optimise campaign impact.Relationship Management: As the ‘in house’ agency for the full Scotland Food & Drink team, the role requires maintaing regular communication with team members and stakeholders, providing updates on campaign status and anticipated resource requirements. Support colleagues in integrating activity across teams to achieve strategic objectives.Brand & Content Management: As the brand ‘guardian’ for Scotland Food & Drink, the role requires ensuring a consistent tone of voice and brand identity across all communication materials, supporting industry leadership positioning and membership engagement. As part of a small, dynamic team, create assets, content and contribute to the development and implementation of content and social media strategies, including website management.Project & Event Support: Collaborate with the Head of Brand and Communications and wider team on large-scale organisational projects, including membership communications, conferences, awards and Showcasing Scotland events. Support planning and content integration for associated digital campaigns.Stakeholder & Community Engagement: Build and maintain relationships with Scotland Food & Drink members and key industry stakeholders. Lead engagement initiatives to foster active online communities and enhance member interaction.Supplier & Agency Management: Oversee relationships with creative agencies and other external suppliers, monitoring performance, addressing gaps and recommending improvements to ensure high-quality delivery.Continuous Learning & Development: Contribute to refining internal processes, templates, and standards to enhance campaign effectiveness and efficiency. Maintain awareness of sector trends, best practices and emerging digital tools to continually improve communications outputs.ABOUT YOUCommunication: Uses clear and effective written and verbal communication skills without supervision, engaging confidently with colleagues, senior stakeholders, suppliers and external partners.Presentation: Works independently to convey messages in a clear, concise and compelling manner and provides guidance to others on effective communication techniques.Confidentiality & Professional Judgement: Works without supervision to exercise discretion and maintain confidentiality when handling sensitive marketing plans, budgets, campaign data or member information.Self-Management & Autonomy: Works without supervision most of the time, to manage workload independently, demonstrating initiative and strong self-motivation and accountability for delivering high-quality campaigns, content, and communications.Attention to Detail: Works with guidance but not constant supervision to ensure all aspects of campaign planning, content creation, digital activity and reporting are accurate, timely and meticulously organised.Problem-Solving: Works without supervision to analyse campaign performance, assess digital and brand metrics and implement effective improvements, escalating issues only where appropriate.Strategic Thinking: Works with guidance but not constant supervision to consider the longer-term impact of marketing activity, aligning campaigns, content and brand strategy with organisational priorities and stakeholder needs. Contributes insight and recommendations that support sustainable and scalable outcomes.Resilience: Maintains effectiveness and composure while managing multiple priorities and changing circumstances.Digital & Marketing Platforms Expertise: Works in collaboration with the wider team to utilise core digital and marketing tools and platforms, including CRM, social media, email marketing, websites, analytics platforms and other relevant technologies.Learning & Development: Works with guidance to maintain and develop professional knowledge in digital marketing, brand management, emerging trends, and sector innovationsExperience: Substantial general work experience together with comprehensive job-related experience in the relevant area to a fully competent level.Qualifications: Degree-level education or equivalent qualification in marketing or communications, digital media, business - desirableABOUT SCOTLAND FOOD & DRINKScotland Food & Drink is the industry leadership organisation tasked with delivering the industry strategy and driving responsible growth for the sector. We are also a membership organisation with over 420+ companies at our heart.Working with us gives you an opportunity to be part of one of the most exciting and innovative industries in Scotland.Our culture and our people are core to delivering our diverse and collaborative work . We have work/life balance as one of our core values and a comprehensive benefits package including, a group pension plan, 35 days annual leave (in addition to your birthday off), enhanced maternity & paternity benefits, a health care cash plan, a flexible working policy, an electric vehicle scheme, a cycle to work scheme, a life assurance scheme, professional study & training assistance, an employee assistance programme and wellness events.There is also opportunities to socialise and attend industry events and dinners.We believe working with us should be a hugely positive experience, our core values are:Ambition – We pursue excellence with unwavering ambition.Collaboration - We believe in the power of working together.Innovation – We embrace a culture of continuous improvement and creativity.Celebration – We believe in recognising and celebrating achievements.Authenticity – We are committed to honesty, transparency, and integrity.Balance – We believe in finding balance between work and the rest of our lives.We are also an equal opportunity employer, and we recognise that diversity and inclusion is a journey, and we are committed to learning, listening and evolving to become more diverse, equitable and inclusive. We support candidates with reasonable adjustments throughout the recruitment process, please let us know when applying if you will require any additional support.APPLY NOWPlease send CV and covering letter, or any questions you have regarding the role to: careers@foodanddrink.scot with the title of the job you are applying for in the subject line.

title: Key Account Manager – Sephora
jd_text: JOB SUMMARY:The Key Account Manager will support the growth and development of L’Occitane at Sephora in the USA and Canada. The ideal candidate is sales-driven, detail-oriented, and a proactive team player. This role is responsible for supporting the Sephora business in alignment with L’Occitane’s brand vision and serving as a key advocate for the business with both internal and external stakeholders. Strong business acumen and the ability to work effectively with Sephora’s corporate support team is essential.JOB RESPONSIBILITIES:Support the Sales Director in managing projects directly related to the Sephora USA and Canada business.Managing communication with Sephora related to assortment changes, new product intake forms, price changes, PDP asset submissions, stock callouts, and day-to-day administrative tasks.Process weekly sales reporting every Monday, including sales performance and product trend analysis recaps.Track and report on sell-in and sell-out performance.Analyze weekly inventory reports to support healthy weeks of supply and drive increased orders to support sell-out.Manage vendor setup and purchase order processing for merchandising, local activations, gratis, and sampling orders.Monitor sales performance and inventory trends to ensure forecasts remain accurate and aligned with the most current business and category trends.Track weekly purchase orders and ensure shipments are executed within the ship window; follow up on delays related to out-of-stocks, chargebacks, or PO discrepancies.Develop and maintain tools to support daily administrative tasks for the Sephora account, with a focus on improving Excel-based reporting and tracking systems.Partner with the Sales Director to coordinate the Field Sales Team in-store resources for store events and training programs to drive sell-out performance.Support the Sales Director in managing sampling initiatives, ratings and review programs, local market activations, and co-op campaigns.Assist the Sales Director in maintaining the marketing calendar, ensuring key dates, programs, and activations are current.Partner with the Sales Director and marketing team to coordinate creative briefs and execute merchandising requests.Conduct regular online site audits, including PDP page reviews and SKU updates.Assist with presentation planning for meetings and external presentations (e.g., Market meetings).Support additional requests from Wholesale team partners as needed.Provide ad hoc business support to the Sales Director as required.KEY PERFORMANCE INDICATORS:Achievement of P&L targets for Sephora USA and Canada, delivering both top-line growth and bottom-line profitability.Sustained sales growth across Sephora USA and Canada, aligned with the three-year plan.Maintain collaborative relationships with key Sephora stakeholders, supporting the Sales Director.Provide actionable insights and identify opportunities for improvement in daily account management operations.Effective ownership and execution of administrative tasks and projects, ensuring accuracy, timeliness, and operational excellence.REQUIREMENTS:EDUCATION:Bachelor’s degree requiredEXPERIENCE:5+ years of documented and quantifiable success in Wholesale Account management and development in a relevant consumer products industry (cosmetics, beauty, skincare, wellness).Sephora experience preferred. SKILLS:Proven track record in sales, account management, and business development within the premium beauty industry.Strong business acumen and deep knowledge of the beauty sector.Highly organized with strong attention to detail.Strategic thinker with the ability to execute tactically.Strong work ethic and self-motivated.Collaborative team player with a supportive management style.Entrepreneurial mindset, with the ability to identify and act on growth opportunities.Strong oral and written communication skills.Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel, and strong skills in Word and PowerPoint.PHYSICAL & TRAVEL REQUIREMENTS:Must be able work in normal office conditions Must be able to work 8 hours per day and be on his/her feet for extended periods of timeMust be able to travel via airplane within the U.S. and Internationally.The compensation for this role ranges from $85K-$90K for the NYC region. Exact compensation will vary based on factors including but not limited to, experience, skills, and geographic location. In addition to annual salary, this position is eligible for participation in a highly competitive bonus program.

title: Entry-level Account Manager
jd_text: We’re looking for an Account Manager who thrives in a client-facing, cross-functional environment and is excited to support customers across both talent solutions and marketing partnerships within commercial real estate. This role begins with deep immersion into SelectLeaders, our industry-leading CRE job board, where you’ll build strong client management fundamentals, understand hiring challenges across the industry, and develop expertise in our platform and customer strategy. As you grow in the role, you’ll expand your portfolio to include Bisnow event and media clients within a dedicated geographic market, creating a unique opportunity to support customers across both hiring and brand visibility initiatives. This is an ideal role for someone who enjoys relationship management, problem-solving, and being at the center of client success while working closely with sales, marketing, and production teams. The position is based in the NYC area. It follows a hybrid schedule: three days in-office at 123 William Street.SelectLeaders Overview:SelectLeaders, a BISNOW company, is the #1 job platform and professional development destination for the Real Estate industry. Our mission is to connect industry-leading employers with their most valuable asset — people — while empowering top-tier talent to advance their commercial real estate careers.Our competitive edge lies in a powerful, continuously expanding network of leading professional real estate organizations. For nearly 20 years, we’ve cultivated relationships that now span professionals who control or influence over 90% of the U.S. commercial real estate market.Key ResponsibilitiesClient Onboarding & SupportAssist with onboarding new clients, ensuring they are set up successfully on the platformProvide day-to-day support to clients, answering questions and resolving issues in a timely mannerHelp troubleshoot job postings, account access, and general platform questionsEnsure a positive and responsive client experience across all touchpointsInbound Requests & CommunicationMonitor and respond to inbound emails, support tickets, and website chatbot inquiriesRoute requests appropriately and follow through to resolutionMaintain clear documentation of client interactionsJob Board Operations & Technical SupportSupport ongoing maintenance of job postings and employer accountsIdentify, resolve, escalate technical issues or bugs to the appropriate teamHelp ensure data accuracy across the platformData, Reporting & InsightsAssist with compiling reports on client activity, job performance, and usage trendsAnalyze data to identify customer patterns and opportunities for improvementSupport leadership with insights that inform product and growth strategyCustomer Trends & Strategic SupportObserve client feedback and recurring questions to help identify product or process improvementsContribute ideas to enhance client experience, candidate flow, and audience developmentAssist with projects related to long-term job board strategy and growth initiativesSales & Account SupportSupport the Enterprise Account Executive with research, proposal preparation, and follow-upHelp manage client requests and ensure timely responsesAssist in preparing materials for client meetings and renewalsBisnow Responsibilities and Job Duties: Work with a positive, cross-functional team on an everyday basis to make certain each piece of inventory that a client purchases is delivered seamlesslyFlawless execution of each sale to ensure client expectations are met, and as frequently as possible, exceededWork with our Bisnow Sales Executives AND Client Success Managers to become the all-important point of contact for all event sponsorship clientsMonitor and care for all post-sale client deliverables: obtaining all necessary digital/event page collateral (eg. photo, bio, logo, ad creative etc.)Own client preparation/sponsor experience leading up to each event: confirming sponsor attendee list, providing event set up instructions, gathering shipping details, etcOwn client preparation/sponsor experience leading up to each ad/content campaign: run dates, deadlines, ad specsCommunicate client needs with ad delivery team, event coordination team, sales team, production team and accountingFollow up post events or digital ad placement to ensure an excellent experienceAnalyze event feedback to ensure each topic is followed up onConstantly innovate our digital and event series, bringing fresh ideas to the table to improve attendee & client experienceProvide feedback from clients to internal departments in an effort to improve Bisnow performanceBuild lasting relationships with all clients that ultimately lead to continued account growthWHAT ARE WE LOOKING FOR?Curiosity about technology, marketplaces, or online platforms. Exposure to technology tools, customer support platforms, or data analysis nice to haveStrong organizational skills and attention to detailExcellent written and verbal communication skillsInterest in partnering with commercial real estate clients within technology, recruiting, and SaaS environments. Demonstrates a customer-first approach and a strong learning mindsetComfort working with data in spreadsheets and systems. Natural curiosity for technology, marketplaces, and digital platforms. Experience with technical tools, customer support software, or data analysis is beneficialAbility to manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced environmentBefore applying, please read our values below. Our values describe and give insight into our culture. If you do not share the same values, this role will not be a mutually beneficial fit. If you do share these values and want to apply, we encourage you to do so!Our values demand that we be curious, self-aware, fearless, consistent. We say yes to seemingly insurmountable challenges because we dare ourselves to push further. And we go the distance because we are individually and collectively entrepreneurial, always asking: Why can’t it be done? We find a way because we care more than the competition. We embrace team and disown ego. We are ruthlessly disciplined and unabashedly kind. We unreservedly challenge the status quo and vigorously fight for new levels of excellence. We believe the difference between good and great is exceptional communication. We obsess over creating value for our team and our customers. We push ourselves and our industry to be more inclusive, to champion diversity and to fight racism, gender bias and all forms of inequality. We never peak. We never quit. We never have excuses. We own our failures and we commit to being smarter and stronger because of them. We give our best today and even better tomorrow. We are here to win.Bisnow will not offer sponsorship for employment status (including, but not limited to, H1-B visa status and other employment-based nonimmigrant visas) for this position. Accordingly, all applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis and must not require Bisnow's sponsorship to continue to work legally in the United States.We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.bonusesGet To Know Our Teams! < Click here for more info about Bisnow, Biscred & SelectLeaders!

title: National Account Manager, E-commerce
jd_text: In 1987, Red Bull not only launched a completely new product, it also created and has led ever since a whole new category, Energy Drinks. Today Red Bull employs around 22,000 people in over 178 countries, selling over 14 billion cans last year. The World of Red Bull provides the forum for you to use your talent and passion, to develop & grow yourself and make an impact through our purpose of giving wings to people & ideas.As National Account Manager - eCommerce you will play a pivotal role in the growth of the channel owning the development and execution of Red Bull Australia’s National eCommerce strategy, establishing new business partnerships through B2B and third-party delivery services, as well as assist in managing key accounts to deliver sales, volume, and perfect online store targets.All the responsibilities we'll trust you with:ACCELERATE ONLINE SALES GROWTH Accelerate growth with aggregators and build the B2B offering through key distributor partners. Manage eComm execution across key accounts (Amazon, Coles, Woolworths) Identify opportunities to partner, activate, share expertise, and drive mutual growth – implementing joint business plans with top customers Adopt a test-and-learn approach, leveraging data to make informed decisions and pivot effectively Drive Red Bull availability by driving distribution in existing customers and driving availability in new marketplacesEXECUTION OF STRATEGIC PROGRAMS AND TRADE INVESTMENT DELIVERY Deliver the monthly business plan financial sales volume in serving units across all key accounts Create and execute promotional programs for new business accounts Manage retail media delivering insight led 360-degree execution by partnering with account teams Provide recommendations to maximise sales effectiveness and ROI from customer activities Keep within approved trade Investment budgets for permanent discounts, temporary price reduction and joint advertising targetsDEVELOP AND EXECUTE NATIONAL ECOMMERCE ACCOUNT PLANS, THROUGH RED BULL ONLINE SUCCESS MEASURE Work cross functionally on further improving and implementing Red Bull’s online merchandising standards Increase Red Bull's online market share through the delivery of a strong joint business plan and increased sales Negotiate trade plans to display an omni channel mindset through key activationsS&OP Understand and adhere to the company S&OP process and metrics, with mandatory participation and engagement in all meetings Provide timely and accurate promotional forecasts, both with the customer, and support internal demand planning process Liaise with finance in providing timely and accurate permanent discounts, temporary price reduction and joint advertising accruals Be responsible for processing claims in the agreed timelines and all financial impacts in relation to responsible accounts Manage the monthly trade spend reconciliation processCROSS FUNCTIONAL ENGAGEMENT Ensure Red Bull looks like number one by working cross functionally with Brand Marketing, NAM’s and Category Development team to execute the ecommerce strategy through key customer moments Be the champion of online execution, working closely with trade marketing and account managers to ensure Red Bull looks like the category leader across all accounts Aid the finance team monthly on accruals and reconciliation of actual to forecast spend Work with the Accounts teams on the delivery of claims processing, payments, and discountsACTIVATION OF THE ‘WORLD OF RED BULL’ IN SELECTED KEY ACCOUNTS Engage customer partners by bringing them into the ‘World of Red Bull” Own key moments and create online events through an end to end online activation program (content, visibility, search). Work with the brand team to develop & execute key occasions across customer portfolioYour areas of knowledge and expertise that matter most for this role:Minimum 5 years of industry experience, including at least 2 years of FMCG key account management experienceAt least 2 years of eCommerce experience in pureplay or omnichannel environmentsExperience in retail media, category management, or trade marketing is highly regardedExceptional negotiation and customer management skillsStrong communication and presentation skillsYou warrant that:You have read and understand the Privacy Statement and the Red Bull Australia Privacy Policy.You are qualified to work in Australia and understand that Red Bull Australia may require you to provide evidence of this to comply with its obligations under the Migration Act 1958 (Cth).All the information you have submitted to Red Bull Australia Pty Ltd (including via this application and any attachments) is true, accurate and complete. You understand that if any information you have provided is false or misleading we may reject your application, review any employment offer made to you and potentially dismiss you from any employment with Red Bull Australia Pty Ltd.

title: Key Account Manager-Pharmacy Channel
jd_text: About The JobJoin Danone, a global leader in health‑focused food and nutritionOwn and grow strategic pharmacy partnerships with Chemist Warehouse and SigmaCompetitive salary, bonus, and excellent employee benefitsAbout the RoleDanone is seeking a driven and commercially minded Key Account Manager – Pharmacy to join our Specialised Nutrition team on a 12months fixed term contract role. This is a pivotal role where you will manage and grow strategic pharmacy customers, delivering strong commercial outcomes while building trusted, long‑term partnerships.Reporting to the Pharmacy Channel Lead, you will be responsible for driving the sales plan, joint business planning, and execution of Danone Nutricia’s Specialised Nutrition and Adult Functional portfolios across Chemist Warehouse and Sigma.Working cross‑functionally with Category, Marketing, Finance, Supply Chain, and Field teams, you will play a critical role in ensuring excellent execution, sustainable growth, and win‑win customer outcomes.Key ResponsibilitiesCustomer & Account LeadershipAct as the primary contact for key pharmacy customers, building strong relationships with senior stakeholdersDevelop and deliver Joint Business Plans (JBP/eJBP) aligned to Danone’s category and channel strategyDrive delivery of financial targets across core pharmacy customers and e‑commerce platformsCommercial & Trade ManagementManage forecasts, trading plans, risks, and opportunities to deliver against annual targetsLead commercial negotiations including promotions, terms, and price changesManage trade spend, customer claims, and investment optimisation using Danone systemsCategory & Execution ExcellenceLead range reviews in partnership with Category teams to secure ranging, shelf, and merchandising gainsDrive best‑in‑class execution of NPD, promotions, and brand activity across in‑store and onlinePartner with third‑party merchandising agencies to ensure strong in‑market executionCross‑Functional CollaborationWork closely with Marketing, Category, Field, Supply Chain, Finance, and eBusiness teamsRepresent customer needs internally and ensure alignment across Danone functionsCulture & Ways of WorkingAct as an ambassador for Danone’s compliance, ethics, and HOPE valuesBring agility, accountability, and collaboration to everything you doAbout youAbout YouYou are a high‑performing Account Manager with a passion for building relationships and delivering commercial results. You enjoy ownership, thrive in fast‑paced environments, and are ready to grow your career within a purpose‑led, global organisation.Ideally, you will have1–2 years’ experience in Key Account or Account ManagementExperience in Pharmacy (Chemist Warehouse, Sigma) preferredStrong commercial acumen with confidence working with numbers and dataProven ability to influence, negotiate, and collaborate with diverse stakeholdersA proactive, resilient mindset with a strong desire to learn and growFormal negotiation training (e.g. GAP Partnership) is advantageous but not essential. About UsWE OFFERWe truly value our Danoners and strive to make their experience exceptional by creating a positive and fulfilling environment for everyone. Our unique benefits are designed to enhance lives and provide genuine value.A high-performing, collaborative team cultureThank Danone it’s Friday! 3pm finishes every FridayDiverse and inclusive work environmentPersonalised learning opportunities to set you up for successCompetitive Total Reward package includingHealth, Life and salary continuance insurance26 weeks gender-neutral paid parental leaveEmployee Share Subscription Plan after 12 months of servicePerformance-related incentives and annual reviewsHow To ApplyIf this opportunity resonates with you and you’re eager to shape and define this role, then go ahead and seize the moment. Click on the APPLY button and share your resume with us.About UsAt Danone, we aim to inspire healthier and more sustainable eating and drinking practices, in line with our Vision – Danone, One Planet. One Health. We operate across four business lines Essential Dairy and Plant-Based Products, Waters, Early Life Nutrition, and Medical Nutrition. title: Corporate Sales - Account Manager - Sydney

We'll get in touch to let you know what the next steps are, but in the meantime feel free to browse this:Why Bloomberg? The BPM / M&E processes ensure real accountability for customer performance at market levelPartner with country in building local Joint business plans & implement at scale in country. Unlock shopper marketing & category platforms to drive value growthSupport Europe digital commerce agenda by embedding digital plans into global negotiations and activationsInspiring Organizations (internal and external)Cluster/Channel heads are aligned to the strategy, resource allocation and executional plans.Country sales teams have the tools, capabilities and support they need to implement perfectly. This customer selects us as their partner of choice.Effective collaboration at all points of the value chain from supply chain to instore activation to data/intelligence & technology.Provides tailor-made assessments on trade terms defensibility supporting with risks and recommendations.Decision Maker ForCustomer / channel plans & strategiesRecommend, lead global customer strategy – directly with global customer teams, indirectly thru country customer/Diageo teamsEuropean JBP resultsPromotional Plan and ConditionalitiesRisk Management (Pricing and Terms)Top to Top contact strategyCustomer negotiation strategiesLeads key account negotiations at Global/pan-European levelOwns M&E and driving value and margin enhancement plans for Global Customer P&Ls. BPM process for international customersStrategy development and business managementDeep understanding of customer P&L, profitability drivers & trade terms structuresConsistent track record to drive RGM/value growth outcomes with large, multi-country customersBrings strategic approach to see and devise effective channel & portfolio plans across customers. Develops effective 3-5 year customer plans and negotiation strategies to increase value for DiageoQuickly builds reputation for expertise in the industry with peers and key influencersAbout YouTop leadership capabilities for this role:Ability to orchestrate a large number of senior stakeholders internally and externally.Demonstrates – and encourages in others - deep personal accountability for great performance and beating the competition.Moves efficiently between customer/brand strategy and operational detailEffectively partners with cross-functional departments (supply, e-commerce, customer marketing, legal, RGM and finance)Creates stretching, yet realistic customer value growth plans. Has ability to reframe value eroding negotiations into growth driving category and portfolio plans.Experience / Skills RequiredStrong and broad track record in commercial with significant tier 1 account handling experience including International Key Accounts (JBP’s), Buying or Service Alliances, P&L accountability and customer marketing experience.Experience in working for – or with – a large leading retailer at national or global levelAn authority in negotiation, resolving issues and creating collaborative relationshipsHas an established network of external relationships - industry peers, key customer contacts and relevant industry authorities.Fluent English. French and/or German speaking desired.Working with usOur purpose is to celebrate life, every day, everywhere. And creating an inclusive culture, where everyone feels valued and that they can belong, is a crucial part of this.We embrace diversity in the broadest possible sense. This means that you’ll be welcomed and celebrated for who you are just by being you. You’ll be part of and help build and champion an inclusive culture that celebrates people of different gender, ethnicity, ability, age, sexual orientation, social class, educational backgrounds, experiences, mindsets, and more.Our ambition is to create the best performing, most trusted and respected consumer products companies in the world. Join us and help transform our business as we take our brands to the next level and build new ones as part of shaping the next generation of celebrations for consumers around the world.Feel inspired? title: Senior Engineering Manager
jd_text: Mistplay is the #1 loyalty app for mobile gamers. Our community of millions of engaged mobile gamers come to Mistplay to discover new games to play and earn rewards. Gamers are rewarded for their time and money spent within the games and can redeem those rewards for gift cards. Mistplay is on a mission to be the best way to play mobile games for everyone everywhere! Download Mistplay on the Google Play Store here and follow us on Instagram, Twitter and Facebook.📍 Please Note: In Canada 🇨🇦, Mistplay follows a 2 days/week in-office hybrid model in Toronto (400 University Ave) & Montreal (1001 Blvd. Robert-Bourassa)As a fast-growing scale-up organization, Mistplay is constantly expanding its user base, product portfolio, and improving its platforms to provide the best possible experience for its users. To achieve these goals, Mistplay is seeking an experienced and motivated Senior Engineering Manager.As the Senior Engineering Manager for Mistplay's Full Stack Mobile App Development team, you will play a critical role in building and growing a successful product and team. The Engineering Manager will be responsible for leading a team of software engineers in the development and maintenance of Mistplay's main product, our Android app. You will work closely with other teams to ensure the app is integrated with the platform's backend, and they will lead the design and implementation of new features and improvements. The successful candidate will have a deep understanding of developing mobile apps/games, as well as experience leading a team of developers in an agile development environment. Additionally, they will be comfortable working with stakeholders to understand business requirements and translating those into technical specifications. What you’ll do at Misplay• Managing an ambitious, Agile, cross-functional, full-stack mobile app development team• Lead the team in the design, development, and maintenance of the mobile application, ensuring that the team delivers high-quality software on time and within budget. You will be responsible for providing technical guidance to the team and resolving technical issues as they arise• Partner closely with stakeholders (e.g Product, Data, Executive) to ensure the delivery of quality features to deliver a fantastic user experience, and guide stakeholders in understanding implementation effort and help guide prioritization efforts• Identify issues by analyzing data, processes, and code to remove blockers, improve delivery and recognize areas for refinement• Continuously review and improve the team's development processes, tools, and practices to increase efficiency, productivity, and quality• Support your team by securing resources, removing blockers, creating alignment and ensuring engagement• Develop and monitor engineering management KPIs to ensure that the team is meeting its goals and that the application is delivering value to users and the business• Act as hiring manager to continue to build out your team to meet changing business requirements, play a key role in recruiting and onboarding new team members, ensuring that new hires are integrated into the team effectively and that the team maintains a high level of diversity and inclusivity.• Write code that is performant, scalable, maintainable and meets functional requirements. You'll have a significant role and voice in technical decisions in partnership with Tech Leads• Stay up-to-date with the latest trends and technologies in mobile app & game development, including Kubernetes, AWS EC2, Beanstalk, DynamoDB, ElastiCache, TypeScript, and modern Android development stack. • You will be responsible for keeping the team up-to-date on these technologies and ensuring that they are used effectively to build the best possible productWhat you’ll bring to Mistplay• Located in Toronto, ON, Canada and open to a 2 days/week hybrid model.• Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science or a related field is preferred• At least 7 years of experience in software development, with a focus on mobile app/game development• Strong knowledge of engineering management KPIs and Agile software development processes and practices• Proven experience in leading and managing high-performing engineering teams, preferably in an Agile environment• Obsession with constant process design and iteration to improve engagement and team productivity• Knowledge of/experience with Kubernetes, AWS EC2, Beanstalk, DynamoDB, ElastiCache, &/or TypeScript• Excellent understanding of software development processes and methodologies• Strong technical background in mobile app/game development, with a deep understanding of the mobile app platforms and its capabilities• Strong experience with software development tools such as Git, JIRA, and Confluence• Ability to work collaboratively with cross-functional teams and stakeholders to identify business needs and translate them into technical requirements• Excellent communication, interpersonal, and leadership skills• Experience with hiring and onboarding new team members• Passion for staying up-to-date with the latest trends and technologies in mobile app/game development, and a desire to continuously learn and improveNice to haves• Knowledge of server-side technologies, such as Node.js, and experience integrating mobile apps with RESTful APIs• Experience in consumer facing/B2C applications, preferably native mobile appsWhy Mistplay?We strive to make our work environment as inviting and fun as possible! Working at Mistplay is coupled with a whole array of perks that we've adopted virtually and in-person: Team Lunches, game nights, company-wide events, and so much more. Our culture is deeply rooted in growth and upheld by a team of smart, dynamic, and enthusiastic people. We utilize data to constantly learn, improve, and adapt. We foster an environment where everyone is encouraged to share their ideas, push boundaries, take calculated risks, and witness their visions come to life.We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Mechanical Engineering Manager
jd_text: Mpac is a global leader in automation and packaging solutions, known for combining advanced engineering with a peoplefocused, collaborative culture. With over a century of innovation, the company designs and supports integrated automation systems that help customers stay competitive across industries such as food, beverage, healthcare, and clean energy.Purpose of the Role:To lead and manage the Mechanical Engineering department by planning and allocating resources, ensuring the delivery of high-quality engineering outputs, and driving the performance, efficiency, and continuous improvement of the team. The role ensures that projects are properly supported, milestones are achieved, engineering standards are upheld, and departmental performance metrics, including utilisation, quality, and process adherence, are monitored and improved. Working closely with Project Management and Controls leadership, the Mechanical Engineering Manager ensures strong cross-functional alignment and contributes to the successful delivery of organisational strategic initiatives.Key Accountabilities And ResponsibilitiesDepartment ResponsibilitiesLead the Mechanical Engineering department, providing strategic direction, resource planning, performance oversight, and operational management. Allocate engineering resources across all mechanical workstreams, ensuring appropriate skills, capacity, and support are available to meet project demand. Monitor and report departmental utilisation, balancing workload across teams and identifying capacity constraints or optimisation opportunities. Oversee the quality of mechanical engineering outputs, ensuring adherence to engineering standards, procedures, and regulatory requirements. Review and track departmental non-conformances and cost-of-poor-quality (COPQ), ensuring timely investigation, corrective actions, and prevention measures. Drive continuous improvement in engineering processes, documentation, design methodologies, and department workflows. Support pre-sales process by reviewing and optimising lead times for prospective projects. Lead the delivery of strategic initiatives and improvement programmes in collaboration with senior leadership. Develop and mentor Team Leaders and senior engineers, fostering talent development and succession planning. Support the creation and maintenance of a positive, collaborative, and high-performance culture within the department. Project & Delivery ResponsibilitiesCollaborate with Project Responsible Engineers (PREs) to ensure progress is on track, engineering plans remain viable, and key milestones will be met. Provide oversight and governance to ensure project designs align with customer requirements, internal standards, and regulatory obligations. Monitor engineering progress across all mechanical work packages, identifying risk areas, resource bottlenecks, or schedule threats and intervening where necessary. Work closely with Project Management to ensure accurate planning, forecasting, and integration of mechanical engineering deliverables within the wider project schedule. Coordinate closely with the Controls Engineering and Electrical teams to ensure seamless interdisciplinary collaboration and best project outcomes. Support root-cause investigation and resolution of technical issues, engineering errors, or design challenges affecting project performance. Provide clear communication to senior leadership and stakeholders on departmental status, risks, performance trends, utilisation, and quality metrics. Knowledge And ExperienceProven experience leading or managing mechanical engineering teams within an automation, machinery, or complex engineered systems environment. Strong background in mechanical design, engineering governance, and adherence to industry standards and regulatory requirements. Demonstrable experience in resource planning, capacity management, and departmental performance tracking. Experience overseeing the delivery of multiple engineering projects simultaneously, ensuring quality and schedule adherence. Solid understanding of engineering project lifecycles, milestone planning, and cross-functional dependencies. Experience managing engineering non-conformances, root-cause analysis, corrective actions, and cost-of-poor-quality reduction. Strong leadership capabilities with experience developing engineering teams, mentoring senior engineers, and fostering high performance. Experience participating in or leading continuous improvement initiatives or large-scale engineering process changes. Technical Skills And QualificationsDegree, HND/HNC, or equivalent qualification in Mechanical Engineering, Mechatronics, or a related engineering discipline. Strong understanding of engineering fundamentals, mechanical design principles, and industry standards relevant to automated machinery. Proficiency with mechanical engineering tools and documentation (CAD awareness, design specifications, technical files), with the ability to evaluate completeness and quality.

title: Engineering Manager, AI Applications
jd_text: About The PositionMeet the Smart Safety CompanyAt Safe Fleet our name says it all. We make fleet vehicles – and everyone in and around them – safer. Our fleet safety platform brings together best-in-class products, ground-breaking technology, and a 100-year history of fleet know-how and innovation to solve the world’s biggest fleet safety problems.Our core value is safety. Without safety first, efficiency and productivity are not possible. This is true for our products, our culture, and our relationship with our community. Our vision is to reduce preventable deaths and injuries in and around fleet vehicles with a goal of ZERO accidents.We are re-defining what safety means for fleets of every type – from school buses to waste collection trucks, firefighting to utility vehicles, police cruisers to delivery vans.Whether you work in our Charlotte plant to build life-saving stop arms for school buses, or design advanced camera vision products in our Vancouver office, forge valves and high-quality nozzles to fight fires, or dream up new ways to protect fleet operators in our Corporate HQ in Kansas City, you’ll contribute to our goal to keep everyone safe.We are a fast-growing manufacturing, service, and technology company with over 1700 employees in over 15 locations across Canada and the US. We’re looking for motivated self-starters with innovative thinking to join our team and help us achieve our growth and performance goals. Sound like you?Job Summary We are seeking a highly technical and hands-on Engineering Manager, AI Applications to lead the design, development, and delivery of scalable AI-powered applications across the organization.This role is primarily focused on building real-world AI applications using modern AI platforms and services (e.g., OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, Google Gemini, Claude, and other emerging AI services). In addition, the role requires solid expertise in Data Science and Machine Learning, ensuring that the team can apply the right approach (whether LLM-based, traditional ML, or hybrid solutions) based on the problem at hand.The ideal candidate combines deep technical expertise in AI systems, strong software engineering fundamentals, and the ability to lead and mentor a growing team. This is not a research-only position, it is a builder and delivery-focused leadership role that also contributes to AI education and enablement across the organization.You will Lead and mentor a team of 4-8 engineers.Design and implement scalable AI-powered applications using modern AI services (OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, Gemini, Claude, etc.), RAG architectures, AI agents, and workflow orchestration.Apply Data Science and Machine Learning techniques where appropriate, including predictive modeling and model evaluation.Build and integrate solutions using API-based LLMs, local/self-hosted models, Hugging Face, transformer-based models, and emerging standards such as MCP.Establish reusable AI/ML architecture patterns, engineering standards, and cost-efficient deployment practices.Contribute directly to prototyping, coding, and production delivery.Translate business needs into structured technical execution plans.Partner with Product and stakeholders to prioritize AI initiatives based on business valueCollaborate with IT, Security, and Compliance to ensure responsible AI deploymentContribute to internal AI training sessions and technical workshops.Promote best practices in AI application design, evaluation, and responsible usage across teams.Salary: $155,000 - $175,000/yr CAD The final offer will be determined based on a combination of factors including skills, experience, and alignment with the role’s responsibilities.Candidates at the lower end of the range will meet the core qualifications and demonstrate strong potential to grow within the role.Mid-range offers reflect a solid match with the role’s technical and collaborative requirements.Offers at the top of the range are reserved for candidates who bring exceptional qualifications, specialized expertise, or unique value that significantly exceeds the baseline expectationsAt Safefleet, we are an equal opportunity employer that is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where everyone is valued and respected. We embrace diversity and do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, or any other legally protected status. We promote fair and equitable hiring practices and foster a culture of inclusivity and respect. Our core values of integrity, innovation, teamwork, customer focus, and safety guide our efforts to provide a workplace where all employees can thrive and reach their full potential.Requirements: 8+ years of software engineering experience, with demonstrated system design expertise2+ years of experience in Engineering Manager, Team Leader or equivalent roles2+ years of hands-on experience building AI-powered applications in productionStrong experience integrating modern AI platforms and services (e.g., OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, Gemini, Claude, or similar)Solid understanding of transformer-based models, prompt engineering, RAG architectures, and AI agentsPractical experience applying Data Science and Machine Learning techniques in real-world scenariosExperience with cloud platforms (Azure, AWS, or GCP)Proven experience leading and mentoring engineering teamsStrong communication skills, with the ability to explain AI concepts to technical and non-technical audiences Preferred Qualifications: Experience working with local/self-hosted models and the Hugging Face ecosystemFamiliarity with MCP and AI agents orchestration frameworksExperience deploying and monitoring ML pipelines in productionFamiliarity with Agile methodologies such as Scrum or KanbanExperience working in regulated or security-conscious environments

title: Engineering Manager
jd_text: Join us in building the future of finance.Our mission is to democratize finance for all. An estimated $124 trillion of assets will be inherited by younger generations in the next two decades. The largest transfer of wealth in human history. If you’re ready to be at the epicenter of this historic cultural and financial shift, keep reading.About The Team And RoleThe Customer Care & Concierge Engineering team builds the products and platforms that support Robinhood’s most engaged customers. This team develops a suite of tools and services—including dedicated support experiences, tax workflows, and estate planning capabilities—designed to help customers manage significant portions of their financial lives with confidence. The work spans customer-facing features and the internal systems that power high-quality service delivery. We are entering a strong growth phase as we expand this offering and continue building toward a comprehensive private family office experience for our customers!As an Engineering Manager, you will lead a team of engineers focused on building and scaling core product capabilities for this premium customer segment. You’ll partner closely with product and design to define and deliver customer-facing solutions from concept through launch, while also strengthening the underlying platform that enables expansion across new geographies. This role is ideal for a technically strong leader who enjoys building 0→1 products, iterating based on customer feedback, and growing engineers early in their careers. You will play a key role in shaping both the product direction and the engineering culture of a team that touches many parts of Robinhood!The role is located in the office location(s) listed on this job description which will align with our in-office working environment. Please connect with your recruiter for more information regarding our in-office philosophy and expectations.What you’ll doLead and develop a team of engineers, providing technical guidance, regular feedback, and support for career growth.Partner with product and design to define requirements, scope milestones, and deliver customer-facing features that support high-value Robinhood customers.Oversee the design and implementation of backend or full-stack systems that power concierge tools, tax-related workflows, and estate planning capabilities.Establish clear engineering processes to ensure reliable delivery, code quality, and system scalability as the product expands to new markets.Contribute technically when needed, reviewing architecture decisions and helping the team solve complex implementation challenges.What you bring1–3 years of experience managing engineers, with a track record of supporting performance and career development.Experience building and launching 0→1 or early-stage consumer-facing products, or leading product-adjacent platform initiatives.Strong backend or full-stack technical background, with the ability to review system design and guide architectural decisions.Experience working on teams that ship customer-facing software at scale, ideally in environments with 50+ engineers.Ability to translate business goals into clear technical plans and measurable milestones.In addition to the base pay range listed below, this role is also eligible for bonus opportunities + equity + benefits.Base pay for the successful applicant will depend on a variety of job-related factors, which may include education, training, experience, location, business needs, or market demands. The expected base pay range for this role is based on the location where the work will be performed.Base Pay RangeToronto, ON$200,000—$235,000 CADClick here to learn more about our Total Rewards, which vary by region and entity.If our mission energizes you and you’re ready to build the future of finance, we look forward to seeing your application.Robinhood provides equal opportunity for all applicants, offers reasonable accommodations upon request, and complies with applicable equal employment and privacy laws. Inclusion is built into how we hire and work—welcoming different backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences so everyone can do their best. Please review the Privacy Policy for your country of application.

title: Engineering Manager
jd_text: Engineering Manager – Operations, RedditchWe’re looking for an Engineering Manager to join our Operations team in Redditch.About SikaSika is a specialty chemicals company with a leading position in the development and production of systems and products for waterproofing, bonding, sealing, damping, reinforcing, and protecting in the building sector and motor vehicle industry. Sika has subsidiaries in 101 countries around the world and manufactures in over 300 factories. Its more than 27,000 employees generated annual sales of CHF 9.3 billion in 2021.Job DescriptionAbout Operations UKOur UK Operations function underpins Sika’s commitment to manufacturing excellence. We deliver safe, reliable, and efficient production across a diverse portfolio of chemical products, ensuring our facilities operate compliantly and sustainably every single day. The team is highly collaborative, focused on continuous improvement, and driven by clear strategic aims: strengthening site safety culture, optimising plant reliability, and embedding robust engineering processes that support long‑term business growth.Purpose of the RoleThis role exists to lead and develop our site engineering function, ensuring our manufacturing assets operate safely, efficiently, and consistently. You will champion a culture where safety is uncompromising, preventative maintenance is embedded, and engineering standards support both current operations and our long‑term strategic priorities.What’s In It for You?The Opportunity:Lead the engineering strategy for a busy production site, ensuring safe and reliable plant performance through robust systems, preventative maintenance, and risk‑based engineering decisions. Drive process improvements that enhance safety, reduce downtime, and elevate operational standards, working closely with Production, HSE, and Quality teams. Shape a proactive engineering culture by coaching and developing a skilled team, ensuring compliance, resilience, and continuous improvement across all engineering activities. The Support:You will join a supportive Operations, HSE, and Engineering leadership community committed to developing safe systems of work, strong asset care practices, and transparent communication. You will have access to digital tools, root cause analysis methodologies, and cross‑functional expertise to help you deliver excellence every day.The Skills:We will support you in strengthening your leadership, engineering management, and safety‑critical decision‑making capabilities. You’ll benefit from tailored development in process safety, lean methodologies, project ownership, and asset‑care excellence—all underpinned by strong technical and leadership learning through the global Sika Business School.A significant commitment to professional and personal development from day one, including a comprehensive local, product knowledge and leadership development initiatives complemented by a global Sika Business School offering.The Future:Potential Career Routes:Engineering Lead, Multi‑Site Engineering Manager, Operations Manager, or wider leadership roles within Sika’s global Operations network.The opportunity to progress within a global company across a variety of business areas.QualificationsWhat We Need From You:• A proven track record in engineering leadership within a manufacturing or process environment—ideally chemicals, FMCG, or related regulated sectors• Strong commitment to safety-first thinking, with experience developing or improving safe systems of work and engineering controls• Excellent understanding of preventative and predictive maintenance practices, reliability programmes, and asset care strategies• Ability to lead, influence, and develop a high-performing engineering team with a structured, process-driven approach• Demonstrable experience managing engineering projects, contractors, and compliance activities (e.g., PUWER, LOLER, pressure systems, ATEX)• Relevant engineering qualification (HNC/D or degree) and a full UK driving licenceAdditional InformationThe Rewards:You will be rewarded with a competitive salary, and a sweep of great benefits. We pride ourselves on our total reward package:• Bonus Scheme• Holiday 25 days rising to 28 days with service, plus bank holidays• Mon – Friday 8am – 4pm• A company pension contribution of up to 10%• Private healthcare for you, with option to add family• Group life cover at 6x your annual salary• Global Income Protection (PHI)• Enhanced maternity and paternity pay• Staff discount platform & Cycle to Work scheme• Dedicated Service Awards for reaching key milestones• Selected discounted Sika productsWe offer competitive salaries, aligned with local market benchmarks and the specific scope and responsibilities of each role. Compensation is determined based skills relevant to the position, education and/or training. We are committed to fair and equitable pay practices in accordance with applicable laws and regulations.

title: Head Of Engineering
jd_text: Join GLS/NXT as our Head of Engineering and take the wheel on one of the most exciting engineering challenges in European logistics.We're building the next generation of logistics technology - scalable, high-traffic products that serve GLS, one of Europe's largest parcel carriers, operating across 40+ countries. At GLS/NXT, you'll work at the intersection of startup speed and enterprise scale - shipping fast, owning outcomes end-to-end, and leading an engineering organisation that genuinely moves the needle.This is a role for a leader who earns trust by example: someone who can mentor and grow engineering managers, bring rigour to how we build and operate software, and be the engineering voice in the room when it matters.TasksPeople & OrganisationLead, mentor, and develop Engineering Leadership and their teams (30-35 engineers total), creating an environment where people do their best work.Own the growth and wellbeing of the engineering org - hiring, onboarding, performance, and retention.Be the engineering culture carrier: fostering ownership, technical excellence, and psychological safety across teams.Engineering Excellence & GovernanceEstablish and continuously improve the standards, processes, and tooling that enable teams to ship reliable, secure, and high-quality software at pace.Own the engineering governance framework: tech stack decisions, cost management, security posture, and architectural standards.Drive cross-team alignment on best practices, ensuring consistency without stifling autonomy.Technical LeadershipBe a credible technical partner - able to get into the weeds on distributed systems, cloud architecture, and engineering trade-offs when teams need it.Evaluate and guide architectural decisions, ensuring our systems scale to meet the demands of high-volume, mission-critical logistics operations.Stay close to the stack without being in the critical path of delivery.Cross-Functional PartnershipCollaborate closely with Product leadership on roadmap planning - contributing the engineering perspective on feasibility, risk, and opportunity.Act as the bridge between business objectives and engineering execution, translating strategy into team-level priorities.RequirementsExperienceProven track record as an Engineering Manager or similar role - ideally in a fast-moving startup or scale-up environment.Experience leading and growing engineering teams of 20+ people, including managing managers.Background in building and operating distributed systems at scale, with a solid understanding of what it takes to run high-traffic, high-availability products in production.Hands-on experience with AWS and modern cloud-native architectures.Leadership & MindsetServant leader at heart - you build trust through listening, removing blockers, and making your teams better.Extreme ownership: you hold yourself accountable and instil that same culture in your teams, without blame or excuses.Strong communicator: you can talk architecture with engineers and strategy with the management in the same afternoon, and you're equally good at both.Data-driven: you make decisions grounded in evidence, not gut feel alone, and you build that habit into your teams.Technical DepthSolid grasp of modern backend engineering: microservices, event-driven architectures, API design, containerisation.Comfortable evaluating engineering costs, tooling choices, and build-vs-buy trade-offs at an organisational level.Security and reliability are not afterthoughts for you they're built in from the start.Why This RoleReal scale, real impact: You'll lead engineering on products used across the GLS markets - one of Europe's largest logistics organisations - meaning your decisions affect millions of parcels and users every day.Greenfield influence: GLS/NXT is still early enough that you'll shape the engineering culture, processes, and architecture - not inherit a rigid legacy.Startup energy, corporate muscle: Move fast and experiment freely, with the stability and distribution muscle of a major European corporate behind you.BenefitsDynamic workplace: A digital environment with international projects and space to learn and growFlexible work conditions: Hybrid working mode with a tailored remote policy.Focus on learning: A personal budget (1.000€) for learning and development initiatives.Physical space for ideas: A great office space in Berlin Mitte with top-tier equipment.Transparent and competitive compensation: A salary package that reflects your expertise and experience.Active participation: A clear bonus system enabling participation in your and the team’s performanceEngaging team culture: Monthly team cooking, regular events and offsitesVacation policy: 30 days of yearly holidayReward system: Rewards for the recommendation of additional team membersAbout GLS/NXTGLS/NXT was founded with the mission to build the next generation of logistic experiences driven by technology - core, adjacent & beyond. More specifically, our sphere of interest is the Logistics and eCommerce sector. We are entrepreneurs and hence always start with the user's needs. We are located between Corporate and Startups and leverage the benefits from both (startup speed and corporate muscle). GLS/NXT inhabits different project teams, acting as startups with business responsibility and setting their own vision and mission. GLS/NXT is part of the GLS IT Services GmbH.Commitment to DiversityGLS/NXT is an equal opportunity employer, which means we do not discriminate on the basis of race, colour, religion, marital status, age, national origin, ancestry, physical or mental disability, medical condition, pregnancy, genetic information, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.
