title: Product Manager / Product Owner
jd_text: About the Company : At Synechron, we believe in the power of digital to transform businesses for the better. Our global consulting firm combines creativity and innovative technology to deliver industry-leading digital solutions. Synechron’s progressive technologies and optimization strategies span end-to-end Artificial Intelligence, Consulting, Digital, Cloud & DevOps, Data, and Software Engineering, servicing an array of noteworthy financial services and technology firms. Through research and development initiatives in our FinLabs we develop solutions for modernization, from Artificial Intelligence and Blockchain to Data Science models, Digital Underwriting, mobile-first applications and more. Over the last 20+ years, our company has been honored with multiple employer awards, recognizing our commitment to our talented teams. With top clients to boast about, Synechron has a global workforce of 14,000+, and has 55 offices in 20 countries within key global markets. For more information on the company, please visit our website: www.synechron.com. SYNECHRON’S DIVERSITY & INCLUSION STATEMENT:Diversity & Inclusion are fundamental to our culture, and Synechron is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. Our Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) initiative ‘Same Difference’ is committed to fostering an inclusive culture – promoting equality, diversity and an environment that is respectful to all. We strongly believe that a diverse workforce helps build stronger, successful businesses as a global company. We encourage applicants from across diverse backgrounds, race, ethnicities, religion, age, marital status, gender, sexual orientations, or disabilities to apply. We empower our global workforce by offering flexible workplace arrangements, mentoring, internal mobility, learning and development programs, and more.All employment decisions at Synechron are based on business needs, job requirements and individual qualifications, without regard to the applicant’s gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, disabled or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Job Title: Product Manager / Product OwnerJob Summary: Seeking a dynamic and experienced Product Manager/Product Owner with 11 years of expertise in digital lending, product lifecycle management, and strategic growth. Responsible for leading end-to-end product development, driving revenue, and optimizing customer experience through innovative digital strategies and agile methodologies.Key Responsibilities:Define and execute product roadmaps aligned with business goals and KPIs.Lead cross-functional teams to deliver innovative product solutions, improving credit processes and customer engagement.Collaborate with stakeholders to gather requirements, prioritize features, and drive product enhancements.Manage product lifecycle from conception to launch, ensuring timely delivery and quality.Drive revenue growth through strategic product management and market analysis.Foster a culture of continuous improvement and agile delivery within the team.Conduct customer research and leverage data-driven insights to inform product strategies.Maintain stakeholder relationships and communicate product performance and roadmaps effectively.Qualifications:11+ years of experience in product management, business analysis, and digital transformation.Proficiency in Agile tools such as JIRA, Confluence, Protecht, Lucid, and Figma.Proven track record in revenue generation, digital lending, and credit process enhancements.Strong leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills.

title: Director Fire Engineering
jd_text: The OpportunityOur Buildings team is seeking Fire Engineering Directors to lead and shape our growing fire engineering capability. These opportunities are national and can be based from any UK office.This is a strategic and client-facing position for a technically exceptional fire engineer who can combine technical authority with business leadership, team development, and market growth. You will help shape the Fire Engineering service while driving high-profile projects to successful delivery.About The BusinessOur Buildings team delivers innovative, sustainable and people-focused design solutions across the UK and internationally. We are expanding our specialist Fire Engineering capability and have exciting opportunities for Associate Fire Engineer Directors to join us.Why Stantec?When we say community, we mean everybody with a stake in the work we do from the clients we collaborate with across many sectors, to the populations we reach, to the thousands of us working together to serve them.Our promise, our values, and our voice support our purpose and guide us in all that we do.We are defined by more than our services. We are defined by what we stand for, what we believe, and why we do what we do. We make a difference in the world by creating communities.This is our purpose, learn more here: Purpose & Values | StantecAbout The RoleStrategic Leadership & Technical Authority: Lead and oversee complex, multi-disciplinary fire engineering projects, ensuring the highest standards of technical excellence. Provide authoritative advice on performance-based design, fire and smoke modelling (CFD), evacuation strategies, and fire safety compliance across diverse building types, including healthcare, life sciences, commercial, residential, and major regeneration projects. Act as the technical sign-off authority for all fire engineering deliverables, ensuring compliance with legislation, standards, and best practice (ADB, BS 9991, BS 9999, NFPA, EN standards). Promote innovation and continuous improvement in fire engineering practices across the business. Client & Market Leadership Build and maintain long-term relationships with senior clients, developers, architects, contractors, regulators, and local authorities. Lead the Fire Engineering team in securing new work, contributing to business development strategy, bids, proposals, and client presentations. Represent Stantec at industry forums, technical panels, and regulatory consultations, enhancing the company’s reputation as a leading fire engineering consultancy. Business Development & Growth Drive strategic growth of Stantec’s Fire Engineering services across UK-wide markets. Identify new market opportunities, emerging sectors, and areas for service expansion. Support leadership in shaping long-term business strategy, service offerings, and commercial success. People Leadership & Team Development Lead, mentor, and develop a team of fire engineers, fostering a culture of collaboration, knowledge-sharing, and technical excellence. Guide staff through professional development, chartership (CEng/IFE) and career progression. Inspire and develop junior and senior staff to achieve project and personal growth objectives. About You Proven track record leading complex, high-profile projects and teams. Strong business development experience, with a history of securing and delivering multi-million-pound projects. Deep technical expertise in performance-based fire engineering, CFD, evacuation modelling, and design solutions. Excellent communication, presentation, and client engagement skills. Experienced in multidisciplinary project environments, able to influence and integrate across Architecture, MEP, Structural, and Project Management teams. Strategic thinker with commercial acumen, strong leadership, and a proactive, collaborative mindset aligned with Stantec values. Qualifications Chartered Engineer (CEng) with IFE membership or equivalent, with extensive experience in fire engineering consultancy. Why Join Us? Our People Culture: We're a close knit team and very proud of our friendly and collaborative environment Awards: Stantec were awarded the Stantec named ‘Best place to work’ and ‘International consulting firm of the year’ at NCE Awards at the 2024 NCE awards as well as being consistently recognised in the top 10 Corporate Knights most sustainable companies in the world. Great Benefits: Competitive salary, pension plan, holidays, free private medical insurance, discounted gym membership and lots more. Flexible working arrangements Great Projects in all of our sectors across the UK&I Industry leading training and development as well as paid for professional subscriptions To hear what some of our employees say about life at Stantec, please click on My Stantec Stories#FireEngineeringAbout StantecThe Stantec community unites more than 34,000 employees working in over 450 locations across 6 continents. We have been working with our clients and communities in the UK for over 150 years. We plan, design, deliver and manage the development and infrastructure needed to support the creation of sustainable, healthy and prosperous communities.Our teams provide effective and relevant solutions, translating our clients’ vision into valued consents, deliverable plans for projects and programmes, and efficient designs for delivery, based on technical excellence and deep market insights. We deal with today’s challenges, but also keep a fairer, better tomorrow in sight, looking at how we deliver clean growth, support radical changes in our economy and meet the needs of future communities.Stantec provides equal employment opportunities to all qualified employees and applicants for future and current employment and prohibit discrimination on the grounds of race, colour, religion, sex, national origin, age, marital status, genetic information, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or gender expression. We prohibit discrimination in decisions concerning recruitment, hiring, referral, promotion, compensation, fringe benefits, job training, terminations or any other condition of employment. Stantec is in compliance with laws and regulations and ensures equitable opportunities in all aspects of employment.Building an inspired, inclusive work environment that attracts, supports, and develops world-class talent is a crucial key to our success. If you are excited by this role but worry that your experience doesn’t exactly align, we encourage you to apply.At Stantec we want you to perform your best at every stage of the recruitment process and are committed to ensuring it is accessible to all. If you need any support or require adjustments to be made then please contact [email protected] and we will talk to you about how we can support you.

title: HR Business Partner/Sr. HR Business Partner
jd_text: SystImmune is a leading and well-funded clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company located in Redmond, WA and Princeton, NJ. It specializes in developing innovative cancer treatments using its established drug development platforms, focusing on bi-specific, multi-specific antibodies, and antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs). SystImmune has multiple assets in various stages of clinical trials for solid tumor and hematologic indications. Alongside ongoing clinical trials. SystImmune has a robust preclinical pipeline of potential cancer therapeutics in the discover and IND-enabling stages, representing cutting-edge biologics development. We offer an opportunity for you to learn and grow while making significant contributions to the company's success.SystImmune is experiencing rapid growth as we advance multiple pipelines in the clinical stage. We are seeking an experienced HR Business Partner to support our corporate, clinical, and scientific organization. This role serves as a strategic partner to clinical leaders while also providing recruiting support as recruiting capacity is being expanded. Over time, the role will transition to focus primarily on HR business partnership, including manager coaching, employee relations, organizational effectiveness, and workforce planning. The ideal candidate is adaptable, self-motivated and comfortable working in a fast-paced biotech environment.This role is full-time onsite at our Redmond, WA location.Key ResponsibilitiesServe as the primary HR partner for assigned teams, providing guidance on performance management, employee relations and manager effectivenessSupport employee relations matters, including investigations and documentationPartner with leaders to identify engagement and retention risks and support change management initiativesSupport onboarding, employee experience, and culture building initiativesProvide recruiting support for clinical roles during the initial growth phase, partnering with recruiters on candidate screening, interview coordination, and hiring decisionsEnsure compliance with employment laws, internal policies, and training requirementsOther responsibilities as assignedQualificationsBachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business, or a related field3+ years of progressive HR experience in biotech or pharmaDemonstrated HRBP or HR Generalist experience supporting corporate, clinical, and scientific teamsPrior experience combining HR partnership responsibilities with recruitingStrong working knowledge of employee relations, performance management and coachingExperience navigating ambiguity and scaling HR practices in a high-growth environmentExcellent interpersonal, communication, and judgment skillsPreferredBilingual in Chinese preferredCompensation and Benefits:The expected base salary range for this position is $100,000 - $150,000 annually. Actual compensation will be based on a variety of factors, including but not limited to a candidate’s qualifications, experience, and skills.While most offers typically fall within the low to mid-point of the range, we may extend an offer toward the higher end for exceptional candidates whose background and expertise exceeds the requirements of the role.SystImmune is a leading and well-funded biotech company with a bright future. We offer an opportunity for you to learn and grow while making significant contributions to the company’s success. SystImmune offers a comprehensive benefits package including: 100% paid employee premiums for medical/dental/vision, also STD, LTD, a 401(k) plan with a 50% company match of up to 3% and a vesting schedule of only 5 years, 15 PTO days per year, sick leave, plus 11 paid holidays and MORE.We offer an opportunity for you to learn and grow while making significant contributions to the company’s success.SystImmune is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We welcome diverse talent and encourage all qualified applicants to apply.

title: Software Developer
jd_text: Our Walsall based client is seeking a permanent Software Developer to join their in-house development team, reporting to the Software Development Team Leader and working closely with experienced engineers and business stakeholders.This is a hands-on role where you’ll design, build, and maintain business-critical systems that support various sites across the UK & Ireland. You’ll play a key part in developing robust, scalable software solutions while following established development standards and best practices.Alongside project work, you’ll provide ongoing support and enhancements for internally developed applications, working collaboratively with end users to understand requirements and deliver effective technical solutions.You’ll be encouraged to build strong working relationships across the business, enabling you to gather requirements clearly, contribute ideas and help shape solutions that meet evolving commercial needs.What You’ll Be Working On The primary focus of this role is our clients EPOS (till) platform, used across all sites in the UK & Ireland.This is a mission-critical system that has been in place for over 25 years and is now being rewritten from the ground up. You’ll be part of the team delivering the next-generation till software — helping to modernise, improve and future-proof a core retail system.Following the initial rebuild, you’ll continue to enhance and maintain the platform, responding to new business initiatives and regulatory requirements.This role offers the opportunity to work on a high-impact system that directly supports store operations and customer experience.Tech Stack (Day-to-Day) Microsoft Visual StudioC# / .NETSQL Server (T-SQL, stored procedures, views)REST APIsJSON and CSV data handlingGit / Azure DevOpsDesirable Experience Includes EPOS or retail systemsFile transfer management (e.g., SFTP)ETL processesFor more information please apply or contact nicole.chapman@clickrecruiting.co.uk directly

title: Data Engineer
jd_text: About UsAt Environment Bank, we’re working together to help restore natural habitats across England. With thousands of acres of new habitat creation already underway, our projects are delivering biodiversity gains and helping vital ecosystems to recover and flourish.Our diverse team includes ecologists, land managers, habitat specialists, geospatial analysts, planning advisors, account managers, digital creatives, legal experts, and more.From our habitat specialists to our sales and marketing teams, everyone at Environment Bank is a champion for our values, working towards our goal of reversing biodiversity loss.Environment Bank is a market-leading organisation in a sector that’s experiencing rapid growth. We’ve already built a highly skilled team of experts and we’re actively looking to expand our team in the coming months.We’re incredibly proud of all we achieved so far and we’re still growing. If you'd like to be part of a driven organisation that's making a real difference, find your next role with the team at Environment Bank.About The RoleThe Data Engineer will play a crucial role in helping the Environment Bank meet its strategic objectives around integration and data reporting. This position is development focused, working closely with teams across the business to streamline data pipelines and improve task efficiency. This individual will focus on setting up automated ETL workflows, analysing data and producing visually appealing and user-friendly reports, and supporting internal and external stakeholders fulfil their data requirements.ResponsibilitiesWork collaboratively on the development of our central cloud-based data platform (Snowflake) Develop and maintain ETL and data integration tools for data flows and pipelines Create and maintain reports and dashboards for a variety of audiences around the business, and the underlying data platform environment Innovate and streamline existing data integrations and processes by identifying gaps and improvements, and designing solutions Mentor and upskill team members Provide data and platform related technical support across the business Create, maintain, and update documentation EDI & BelongingAt the heart of Environment Bank are the people, who make it all possible. We employ individuals, who share the same passion to live our Environment Bank mission and values.At Environment Bank we believe that fostering a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace makes us more innovative, dynamic, and competitive. We welcome individuals from all backgrounds, ethnicities, cultures, and experiences. We are committed to creating an environment where everyone is valued and respected, free from discrimination based on race, religion, sexual orientation, age, marital status, disability, gender identity or expression, or any other legally protected status.RequirementsEssentialSelf-starter with curious mindset, a desire to help colleagues through problem solving and learning new skills Desire to be part of a team supporting our nature recovery work Experience with programming languages (Python, SQL) maintaining, creating or integrating APIs and data modelling Experience with ETL and data platform integrating (Microsoft Power Platform, Azure, APIs, custom code integrations) Experience with Snowflake cloud data platform and the development environment Experience visualising data using dashboards to communicate to a variety of audiences (e.g. Streamlit) Strong understanding of data management, version control (GitHub) and quality assurance/ quality control Strong attention to detail, time management and organisational skills Ability to work collaboratively and independently as part of a fully remote team Ability to manage multiple tasks and projects in fast-paced environment Bachelor’s degree in a relevant or related field (e.g. Computer Science, Data Science, Maths etc.), or equivalent professional experience or qualification DesirableExperience within the ecology or environmental sector, with an awareness of Biodiversity Net Gain and nature restoration Understanding of spatial data integration Experience with our other wider systems architecture (e.g. HubSpot, Asana, Azure, and others) BenefitsAs an organisation, we believe that work should be fulfilling, inspiring, and balanced, so we’re creating a space where our team can thrive, both personally and professionally – providing flexible working arrangements, mental wellbeing support, and more.We offer...Performance-based annual bonus up to 10% of salaryPension scheme with employer-matched contributions at 6% of salary️ 30 days of annual leave, plus paid bank holidays, birthday, and wellbeing day➕ Option to purchase 5 additional days of annual leave after probationRemote and hybrid flexible working optionsRegional and departmental team co-working daysExpenses-paid Whole Company Meet Twice a Year️ Team fundraising and charity eventsOngoing career development with training and mentoringMedical cash back scheme to cover everyday healthcare costs‍ Employee assistance mental wellbeing support❤️‍️ Enhanced sickness pay allowance

title: Test And Integration Engineer - ESN
jd_text: Location – Bristol (frequent travel across the UK will be required)Why this job mattersThis role supports the successful integration of new systems, products, services, and validation campaigns to assure the quality of the deliveries on behalf of BT/EE for the Emergency Services Network (ESN) and its customers, through technical and commercial launch.The Emergency Services Network (ESN) is a major strategic priority for BT and our commitment to do the best for our customers and our country. ESN will be the most advanced Emergency Services Network in the world – BT/EE will be helping Britain to be a world leader in mobile. Growing coverage, enhancing reliability and increasing resilience will be the most important elements of building this vital communications network, which the Emergency Services will rely on to save lives and protect us all. The ESN will replace the existing Airwave network and will be built on the existing 4G/5G network and will provide essential voice, data and video services as well as critical communication capability to 385,000 of Britain's Emergency Services Users (Fire and rescue, Police and Ambulance). Our consumer and business customers will benefit from expanded 4G/5G coverage and enhanced network reliability and resilience. The Senior Test Engineer will have strong detailed technical skills, experience in and knowledge of end to end integration testing in both Reference as well as Production environments. Being able to demonstrate knowledge preferably across multiple domains such a RAN, Cloud core, IMS and or applications. The role is part of a growing team of on-shore senior test engineers.The Senior Test Engineer will be responsible for supporting the delivery of the EE/BT Programme Test strategy, Testplans and Test case documentation as well as be accountable for test execution, and responsible for progressing tes case automation, trouble shooting and reporting. Our purpose is to use the power of communication to Connect people for good. Act as an advocate for Quality Assurance and Integration, engaging other functions within Technology and across functions, in order to communicate test principles, aims and process. Share success and ensure the value added is understood by technical and non-technical stakeholders.You will be required to undergo National Security Vetting to SC or NPPV level.What You’ll Be DoingDesign Test Cases and Support Creation of test plans, embedding automation throughout.Champion Quality Assurance and Integration across BT Technology.Execute end-to-end testing and integration, ensuring alignment with standard such as TMMi and ISTQB standards as required.Report test results and test issues following established defect management processes, adhere to agreed ways of working and governance always striving to achieve the highest quality output.Build and maintain strong relationships with internal teams and suppliers and where relevant Home Office teams and emergency services users.Support and execute integration testing, Support User acceptance and Operational Acceptance testing as well as relevant Non-functioanl testing such as performance and fail over testing through to live validation.Support the successful resolution of testing-related issues.Provide analysis to support informed decision-making with customer experience in mind.What We Would Like To See On Your CVHands-on experience executing testing on core network or radio network, with 4G testing experienceExperience with Nokia and Ericsson RAN vendorsUnderstanding of test methodologies and practices (ISTQB)Proven experience deriving test cases from requirements and executing themExperience analysing test results and test automation experience (automating test cases and running in automated frameworks)Must have Excel, NetScout, Jira and Open-source automation tooling knowledge and experience.The Skills You’ll NeedTest PlanningTest ManagementDecision MakingBenefitsAt BT, we entertain, educate, and empower millions of people every single day. We’re a brand built on connecting people – whether that’s friends, family, businesses, or communities. Working here, you’ll receive an attractive salary and a range of competitive benefits, but – more than that – you’ll be joining an ambitious organisation with a culture of togetherness, collaboration, and inclusivity, that takes a genuine and proactive interest in your progress and development. 10% on target annual bonus BT Pension scheme, minimum 5% employee contribution, BT contribution 10% X4 Salary Life Assurance Huge range of flexible benefits including Cycle to Work, Healthcare, Season Ticket Loan, Electric Vehicle Salary Sacrifice 25 days annual leave (not including bank holidays), increasing with service From January 2025, equal family leave: receive 18 weeks at full pay, 8 weeks at half pay and 26 weeks at the statutory rate. It’s for all parents, no matter how your family is made up. Enhanced women’s health support: including help with menopause symptoms, cancer screenings, period care and more. 24/7 private virtual GP appointments for UK colleagues 2 weeks paid carer’s leave World-class training and development opportunities Option to join BT Shares Saving schemes Discounted broadband, mobile and TV package Access to 100’s of retail discounts including the BT shopFlexible WorkingThis role offers a 3 together, 2 wherever working model. This means you are required to be at your contractual location 3 days a week with 2 flexible days per week.With over 175 years of heritage, BT is now the flagship business brand of BT Group. We’ve brought together our best people and capabilities into a B2B powerhouse serving 1 -2 million business customers internationally.We’re a global leader for secure connectivity and collaboration platforms for businesses of all shapes and sizes, from big household names and government departments, right through to sole traders and new start-ups. But it’s not just the technology that matters, it’s what it can do to help them build stronger, smarter, more secure businesses.We value diversity and inclusion and believe in making a positive impact. We connect for good by championing digital inclusion and equipping people, businesses, and communities with digital skills to thrive.As a member of our team, you will be part of an organisation that celebrates difference, fosters innovation and provides you with opportunities to be your best. With millions of businesses relying on us daily, joining BT means you can be part of a diverse and multi-skilled team that makes a significant impact to society.A FEW POINTS TO NOTE:Although these roles are listed as full-time, if you’re a job share partnership, work reduced hours, or any other way of working flexibly, please still get in touch.We will also offer reasonable adjustments for the selection process if required, so please do not hesitate to inform us.DON'T MEET EVERY SINGLE REQUIREMENT?Studies have shown that women and people who are disabled, LGBTQ+, neurodiverse or from ethnic minority backgrounds are less likely to apply for jobs unless they meet every single qualification and criteria. We're committed to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace where everyone can be their best, so if you're excited about this role but your past experience doesn't align perfectly with every requirement on the Job Description, please apply anyway - you may just be the right candidate for this or other roles in our wider team.

title: Software Developer
jd_text: At ClearSky Logic, we are The Tech Enabler, unlocking exponential business growth. Our mission is to work alongside our clients to solve the most complex challenges, create undeniable value, and set a new standard for what is possible in the industry.Companies like Inizio, Startline Motor Finance, and Multrees trust us to get the job done and now, we're growing our team to take things even further.Role DetailsLocation: Glasgow- SkyPark- Hybrid (3 days on site)Salary: up to 52,5K up to 10% company bonusPlease note: We are unable to offer sponsorship.How We Support Our Team34 days holiday - because work-life balance matters.Birthday off - no one should work on their special day.Health and Security - Sick pay, private dental & medical insurance - we've got your health covered.Family First - Enhanced family-friendly policies.Growth Path - Tailored professional development - we support your growth so you can become the expert you're meant to be.Culture Matters - Office perks & team celebrations.Your Proactive Role in the SquadWe are looking for a TypeScript specialist to join our team. We operate in a unified TS ecosystem, using React on the frontend and NestJS on the backend. You will be responsible for building high-performance UIs while having the autonomy to jump into the backend to manage DTOs and ensure end-to-end type safety.Technical Requirements:TypeScript: Deep knowledge of advanced TS patterns (Generics, Type Guards, Mapping). You should be comfortable architecting shared type libraries that synchronise our React frontend with our NestJS API.Modern React: Solid understanding of the React lifecycle, including custom hooks, performance profiling (DevTools) and efficient rendering strategies.State & Data Flow: Experience with state management (Zustand/Redux) and data-fetching libraries like React Query or RTK Query, specifically focusing on how they improve efficiency so as not to perform unnecessary fetches.Real-time Interaction: Implementation of WebSockets or Server-Sent Events for real-time updates.Backend awareness - Being able to navigate the full stack and translate DTO’s when backend resources are at capacity.Autonomous: Be able to manage your own workload, provide rough estimations of completion and develop complex UI based on provided designs.Why Join CSL?We are here to support our people in breaking barriers and delivering game-changing solutions. You'll work in an environment where your initiative, expertise, and drive will make a real difference, fostering your own and our clients' growth.

title: Testing Engineer (vehicles / Engines)
jd_text: Job DescriptionPosition: Testing Engineer (Vehicles / Engines)Location: NailsworthEmployment Type: Full-timeSalary Range: £ (dependent on experience)Working Hours: Monday to Thursday, 7:30am - 4:30pm, 7:30am – 2:00pm Early Finish Friday (40-hour week with occasional weekend work)About Fluid Transfer InternationalFluid Transfer International is a leading designer and manufacturer of innovative refuelling solutions for the aviation, military, and marine industries. Established in 1967, we have delivered world-class refuelling systems for over five decades, supported by our commitment to unparalleled service and support.Position OverviewWe are seeking a Testing Engineer to join our growing team at our Nailsworth site. This role offers exceptional training and development opportunities within a company that serves the aviation sector with specialist engineering solutions.This position is ideal for both mechanically and electrically biased candidates at various experience levels who are eager to develop their skills in a supportive, technical environment.Key Responsibilities· Carry out vehicle testing and repair on specialist equipment· Work with hydraulics, pneumatics, electrical systems, and engines· Support the service and after-sales team as required· Visit customer sites for on-site testing, installation, and calibration· Collaborate effectively within a small, skilled engineering team· Maintain high standards of workmanship and quality controlRequired Skills & ExperienceEssential:· Mechanical or electrical engineering background· Hands-on practical experience· Strong interest in technical systems and problem-solving· Ability to work effectively both independently and as part of a team· Willingness to travel occasionally to customer sites Highly Advantageous:· Experience working with engines· Knowledge of hydraulic and pneumatic systems· Understanding of specialist vehicle systems All experience levels welcome to apply - comprehensive training will be provided to the right candidate.Compensation & BenefitsPay & Incentives:· Hourly rate: £ (dependent on experience)· Overtime opportunitiesBenefits Package:· 23 days annual leave plus bank holidays o Increasing to 24 days after 5 years' serviceo Increasing to 25 days after 10 years' service· Company pension scheme· Life insurance· Free on-site parking· Company Benefits Portal offering Discount and Health and Wellbeing Support Professional Development:· Fantastic training and development opportunities· Work with industry-leading specialist equipment· Exposure to global aviation industry projects· Career progression within a growing company· Mentorship from experienced engineering professionalsWorking Arrangements· Schedule: Monday to Thursday, 7:30am - 4:30pm, 7:30am – 2:00pm Early Finish Friday (40 Hours) with overtime available · Flexibility: Occasional weekend work may be required· Location: Primarily based at Nailsworth site with occasional travel to customer locations for installation, testing, and calibration workWhy Join Us?· Work with a company that has a global reputation in the aviation industry· Gain experience with specialist, cutting-edge equipment· Develop skills across multiple engineering disciplines (hydraulics, pneumatics, electrical systems, engines)· Be part of a small, collaborative team environment· Excellent training and career development pathway· Contribute to critical projects supporting the aircraft industryApplication ProcessWe welcome applications from candidates with either mechanical or electrical backgrounds and at all experience levels. If you have hands-on experience and a passion for engineering, we encourage you to apply.We are committed to developing our employees and providing opportunities for career advancement. Join our team and build your engineering career with an industry leader.

title: Senior Product Manager, Card
jd_text: Our Product Management team drives product initiatives from inception through execution, makes impacts and launches products/features across our global footprint. We collaborate closely with engineers, designers, analysts, marketers, and other cross-functional team members to ship remarkable features and products.As a Card Product Manager, you will be responsible for the end-to-end management of Crypto.com card products. You will work closely with cross-functional teams including marketing, finance, risk management, and technology to ensure the success of card products and drive revenue growth.ResponsibilitiesIdentify the most appropriate way to launch in a new country and manage the process end-to-end which includes critical stakeholder management and understanding of local regulatory requirementsBe accountable for designing an achievable timeline and the delivery of the product into marketManage and improve relationships with business partners/ vendors (card schemes, issuers, acquirers etc.) to work out the best solution and commercial agreements for Crypto.comObtain a detailed understanding of the product and help execute enhancements that aim at improving Crypto.com's market penetration, usage and profitabilityLead cross-regional and cross-functional project teamsKeep updated on current trends, competitors and developments in both the crypto and financial marketRequirementsBachelor's degree in business, finance, marketing, or a related field8+ years of experience in product management, preferably in the financial services industry with a focus on card productsStrong understanding of card payment systems, regulations, and industry trendsProven track record of successfully launching and managing card products that drive revenue growthExcellent analytical, problem-solving, and project management skillsEffective communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate cross-functionally and influence stakeholders at all levelsExperience with agile product development methodologies is a plus #LI-JuniorLife @ Crypto.com Empowered to think big. Try new opportunities while working with a talented, ambitious and supportive team.Transformational and proactive working environment. Elevate employees to find thoughtful and innovative solutions.Growth from within. We help to develop new skill-sets that would impact the shaping of your personal and professional growth.Work Culture. Our colleagues are some of the best in the industry; we are all here to help and support one another.One cohesive team. Engage stakeholders to achieve our ultimate goal - Cryptocurrency in every wallet. Are you ready to kickstart your future with us? Benefits Competitive salary Medical insurance package with extended coverage to dependents Attractive annual leave entitlement including: birthday, work anniversaryWork Flexibility Adoption. Flexi-work hour and hybrid or remote set-upAspire career alternatives through us. Our internal mobility program can offer employees a diverse scope. Work Perks: crypto.com visa card provided upon joining Our Crypto.com benefits packages vary depending on region requirements, you can learn more from our talent acquisition team. About Crypto.com: Founded in 2016, Crypto.com serves more than 80 million customers and is the world's fastest growing global cryptocurrency platform. Our vision is simple: Cryptocurrency in Every Wallet™. Built on a foundation of security, privacy, and compliance, Crypto.com is committed to accelerating the adoption of cryptocurrency through innovation and empowering the next generation of builders, creators, and entrepreneurs to develop a fairer and more equitable digital ecosystem. Learn more at https://crypto.com. Crypto.com is an equal opportunities employer and we are committed to creating an environment where opportunities are presented to everyone in a fair and transparent way. Crypto.com values diversity and inclusion, seeking candidates with a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and skills that complement and strengthen our team. Personal data provided by applicants will be used for recruitment purposes only. Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.We may use artificial intelligence tools to analyze the content of your Resume/CV against the specific requirements for the position. The purpose is to support our recruitment team in reviewing applications more effectively. These tools assist our recruitment team in their evaluation of your application by providing recommendations, but they do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans who consider the insights generated by the tools along with other relevant information. If you would like more details about how your personal information is processed, please contact us.

title: Director Of Engineering
jd_text: About GlossGeniusGlossGenius is building an ecosystem enabling entrepreneurs to succeed. We empower small business owners to focus on being creators, not admins, by offering a range of business management tools including booking and scheduling, marketing, analytics, payment processing and much more.Over 100,000 small business owners have chosen to rely on GlossGenius every day to run their entire set of business operations. Joining its powerful, intuitive platform with its vibrant, distinguished brand, GlossGenius is the ideal combination of a fintech, SMB software, and consumer company all in one.About the RoleGlossGenius has been empowering independent entrepreneurs in the beauty and wellness space to pursue their dreams and successfully grow their businesses. As we continue to scale, we're strengthening our engineering organization. As a Director of Engineering at GlossGenius, you will lead multiple teams critical to delivering value to our 100,000+ business owners.You'll oversee the engineering leaders that represent our core product pillar. Your teams will work across backend, frontend, and mobile stacks to deliver scalable, high-quality solutions. Whether driving features that directly impact our customers' success or enabling engineering velocity and reliability across the organization, your leadership will be key to helping GlossGenius move faster, build better, and scale with confidence.As a Director, you'll set execution standards across your pillar, develop 1+ year technical strategy aligned with business goals, build and coach a team of managers, and serve as a key voice in engineering leadership. You'll be responsible for technical strategy, organizational development, and ensuring alignment between engineering execution and business objectives.We default to being in-office 3-4 days per week with required attendance on Tuesdays and Thursdays.What You'll DoExecution & DeliveryLead and develop a team of engineering managers and their senior and staff engineers, fostering a culture of excellence, growth, and high performance across multiple teamsOwn the delivery of major initiatives across multiple teams, ensuring cross-functional coordination, risk management, and successful business outcomesEstablish engineering standards, processes, and best practices that scale across the organization while maintaining quality, velocity, and developer experienceDefine and track key engineering metrics and OKRs, using data to drive continuous improvement in team performance and deliveryVision & StrategyPartner with senior leadership, product, and design to define multi-year technical and product strategy, driving organizational alignment on priorities and execution roadmapsDrive technical architecture decisions and trade-offs that balance immediate business needs with long-term scalability and maintainabilityLeadership & Team BuildingBuild organizational capacity by partnering with recruiting to attract, hire, and retain top engineering talent, while developing succession plans and career frameworksMentor and coach engineering managers, helping them develop their leadership skills and technical judgmentNavigate organizational dynamics and build coalitions, providing clear communication to executive stakeholders on progress, risks, and technical strategyWhat We're Looking For8+ years of software engineering experience, including 4+ years in engineering leadership roles (managing managers)Proven track record of scaling engineering teams and building high-performing organizations of 50+Deep experience collaborating with executive leadership, product, and design to develop strategy, manage stakeholder expectations, and deliver complex, multi-team initiativesStrong business acumen and customer-centric mindset, with the ability to translate business objectives into technical strategySolid technical depth across modern engineering practices, architectures, and systems, with the ability to evaluate technical decisions and guide senior technical leadersDemonstrated success in recruiting, developing, and retaining exceptional engineering talent, including building diverse and inclusive teamsExceptional communication and influence skills, with experience presenting to executives and rallying large groups around shared visionExperience managing distributed or hybrid teams preferredThe starting base salary for this role in New York is between $272,000 - $340,000 + target equity + benefits. The base salary offered is dependent upon many factors including skills, experience, location, and education. The base pay range is subject to change and may be modified in the future. Additionally, this role is currently eligible to participate in GlossGenius’s equity plan as well as a range of health & wellbeing, retirement savings, and other benefits within our total rewards offering.At GlossGenius, we celebrate our differences and are committed to creating a workplace where all employees feel supported and empowered to do their best work. We believe this benefits not only our employees but our product, customers, and community as well. GlossGenius is proud to be an Equal Opportunity and Affirmative Action Employer.Personal Information: Notice at Collection for Employees and ApplicantsAgency SubmissionsIf a resume or applicant is submitted to GlossGenius by a third party without a signed search agreement in place, it will become the property of GlossGenius and no fee will be paid, irrespective of whether the candidate is hired. These AI Tools may be used alongside human review during one or more stages of the recruiting process, including application screening, skills assessments, and interviewing. title: HR Business Partner
jd_text: Job Description - Human Resource Business PartnerWe are an electronic retailer of jewelry and lifestyle products headquartered in Austin, Texas with a passionate team of approximately 500 people. Our products are sold through TV on 80m homes through our live 24*7 broadcasts, our website, OTT, marketplaces, and social media.We are the US retailing unit and subsidiary of Vaibhav Global Limited (VGL) which is a well-established vertically integrated company, founded in India about 35 years ago and listed on the Indian stock exchanges. The company has had a turnover of $246 million (in the financial year 2022).The other retailing units of VGL Group are in the UK (Shop TJC) and Germany (Shop LC, Germany). Together, all the units ship out approximately 50,000 pieces of products to their customers in the US, UK, and Germany every day. In addition to the companies in the US, UK, and Germany, VGL Group operates offices globally in India, China, Indonesia, and Thailand.We believe in supporting our community and protecting the environment with the purpose of Delivering Joy.Our service to the community is run through our Your Purchase Feeds… program under which each retailing unit donates one nutritious meal to a school-going child in India, the US, and the UK for each item sold through all its platforms. Your Purchase Feeds… has provided over 40 million meals to kids until now in the US. Our mission statement embraces the ambitious goal of distributing one million meals each day to children in need by 2040. We believe that Your Purchase Feeds… provides more than just a meal: it feeds the hearts, minds, and futures of children.We are committed to protecting the environment by constantly working on reducing our carbon footprint and becoming net zero by 2032. Our offices in India function on solar power, and each business unit is working towards getting a LEEDS certification.We live by and are inspired by our core values of Teamwork, honesty, commitment, passion, and a positive attitude, which form the basis of every decision we make.Hr Business Partner – Role OverviewShop LC is looking to add a dynamic, knowledgeable, and proactive HR Business Partner to our amazing HR team! As an HR Business Partner, you will need general working knowledge of common HR policies & procedures, as well as compliance measures. Most importantly, we are looking for someone who is solutions-oriented and enjoys digging- in to root causes to build better processes and teams!The ideal HR Business Partner should have a passion toward people development, employee relations and performance management. This position will report to the Director of HR and work closely with other HR Business Partners, often collaborating and supporting the areas of compensation, benefits, talent acquisition and HR process improvement.Work Location – 100 Michael Angelo Way, Suite 300, Austin, Texas 78728Reporting Manager – HR DirectorJob ResponsibilitiesServe as a point of contact for employee concerns, resolving issues related to performance, behavior, and conflict resolution.Support overall employee experience, from onboarding and new hire training experience to career progression and placement, including creating and leading training programs.Lead engagement initiatives and retention by initiating and analyzing employee feedback, opinion surveys, and exit interviewsLead or participate in organizational change initiatives, ensuring smooth transitions and alignment with business goals.Build strong relationships with key stakeholders, acting as a trusted advisor and influencing decision-making processes at the highest levels of the organization.Collaborate with business leaders to understand their goals and develop HR strategies that support organizational objectives.Provide guidance on workforce planning, talent management, and organizational design to optimize business performance.Provide advice, feedback, and suggestions to assigned department managers on a daily/weekly basis around employee relations and performance management issuesDrive consistency and support with managers’ monthly 121 meetings, reviews, and feedback tools.Assist in the development, documentation, and implementation of human resource policies.Gather and analyze data with useful HR metrics; demonstrate the ability to make informed decisions based on data and statistics.Maintain confidential employee files and records in electronic and paper formEnsure HR compliance standards are met and followed, including but not limited to work with Unemployment claims, EEOC charges, WOTC, and FLSA regulationsComply with company policies and procedures, while looking at ways to improve the overall Employee experienceAll other duties as assigned by the managerSkills/ExperienceBackground of 5-7 years in HR (Degree is preferred but not required)Proficiency in Spanish language is preferred and considered a strong plus.Familiarity with HRIS and general payroll practices (e.g. ADP)Understanding of HR policies and procedures and understanding of Texas Employment/ Labor LawsExperience in Employee Relations, Investigations, Performance Management, Talent Development, and coaching leadersExcellent communication and people skills, demonstrates approachability and workload organization, with the ability to follow up on various prioritiesAptitude in empathy, problem-solving and critical thinking; able to adapt to fast-paced 24/7 home shopping network environmentSelf-Driven, with a desire to work as a team and towards measurable resultsOutstanding knowledge of MS Office (Excel and PowerPoint specifically)Ambassador of our Core Values: Teamwork, Honesty, Commitment, Passion, and Positive AttitudePhysical RequirementsRemaining in a stationary position- often standing, or sitting for prolonged periodsAdjusting or moving objects up to 40 pounds in all directions, at times may need to reach above heard or use a ladder to bring files or supplies downRepeating motions that may include the wrists, hands, and/or fingers (often in front of a computer)Benefits of working with usCulture of openness and flexibilityBalanced work lifeOpportunity to make a difference in the communityAttractive pay and benefits including:Company-paid, employee-only medical, dental, vision, and life insurance. 4% 401(k) matching.Company profit sharing.Up to 15 days of PTO & vacation time, maternity/paternity leave, company-covered short-term & long-term disability, paid training time, paid volunteer time, 1 floating holiday and 6 paid holidays.Corporate discounts & employee sales & discounts.Tuition reimbursement and scholarship (for dependents going to college).Employee Assistance Program.

title: HR Business Partner Sr. Manager
jd_text: WHAT YOU DO AT AMD CHANGES EVERYTHINGAt AMD, our mission is to build great products that accelerate next-generation computing experiences—from AI and data centers, to PCs, gaming and embedded systems. Grounded in a culture of innovation and collaboration, we believe real progress comes from bold ideas, human ingenuity and a shared passion to create something extraordinary. When you join AMD, you’ll discover the real differentiator is our culture. We push the limits of innovation to solve the world’s most important challenges—striving for execution excellence, while being direct, humble, collaborative, and inclusive of diverse perspectives. Join us as we shape the future of AI and beyond. Together, we advance your career. The RoleThe HR Business Partner Sr. Manager will partner closely with senior executives in G&A and take full ownership of strategic HR and talent initiatives. Success in this role requires a strong executive presence, consultative depth, and the ability to influence outcomes at the highest levels of the organization.The PersonYou are recognized by leadership for your depth of business insights and strategic HR Business Partnership and have the credibility and confidence to engage, challenge, and guide senior executives. You bring a consultancy mindset, grounded in business acumen and data-driven insight, and thrive in environments where ambiguity and transformation are the norm.Key ResponsibilitiesKey capabilities include HRBusiness Mindset: Thinks “big picture.” Approaches work with a long-term, enterprise-wide perspective. Connects business strategy with people priorities, using data-driven insights to inform decisions. Understands market trends, competitive dynamics, and internal metrics to connect the dots, see patterns and tell a clear story, and drive organizational success.Org & Talent Enabler: Builds strong, effective partnerships with leaders to shape high-performing teams and organizations. Provides strategic guidance on workforce planning, succession, and team effectiveness. Fosters a culture of inclusion, engagement, and continuous improvement while navigating matrixed environments with confidence and agility. Operates with confidence, curiosity to innovate and discipline to execute.Change Leader: Anticipates and diagnoses organizational challenges and leads innovative solutions that drive alignment, efficiency, and transformation. Guides teams and individuals through change with empathy and clarity, actively listening and creating trust and engagement in dynamic, fast-moving environments.Active Influencer: Brings a strong sense of ownership and a bias for action. Humble leader who influences outcomes through data and people insights and relationship-building, while championing collaboration and inclusive thinking. Drives execution excellence and meticulously plans, prioritizes, and executes actions with precision and effectiveness.Preferred ExperienceDeep expertise in org effectiveness, leadership coaching, and talent strategy execution. Prior G&A HRBP consulting experience a plus Proven track record of leading through change and delivering results with precision & impact.Ability to work in sophisticated, global, matrixed organizationsExceptional interpersonal and relationship-building skills, with the ability to engage and collaborate effectively at all levels of the organization.Highly organized and self-driven, capable of managing multiple priorities with flexibility and strong follow-through.Not afraid to roll up your sleeves up and get involved - no job is too big or too small.Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint, with the ability to create compelling, data-driven insights and executive-ready presentations.Academic CredentialsBachelor's degree in related discipline preferred. This role is not eligible for visa sponsorship.Benefits offered are described: AMD benefits at a glance.AMD does not accept unsolicited resumes from headhunters, recruitment agencies, or fee-based recruitment services. AMD and its subsidiaries are equal opportunity, inclusive employers and will consider all applicants without regard to age, ancestry, color, marital status, medical condition, mental or physical disability, national origin, race, religion, political and/or third-party affiliation, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, military or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We encourage applications from all qualified candidates and will accommodate applicants’ needs under the respective laws throughout all stages of the recruitment and selection process.AMD may use Artificial Intelligence to help screen, assess or select applicants for this position. AMD’s “Responsible AI Policy” is available here.This posting is for an existing vacancy.

title: Product Manager
jd_text: About ArlulaArlula is small, scaling fast, and building infrastructure relied on for climate and national security resilience. We provide the infrastructure that connects sensors in space to the people and algorithms that use that data on the ground. Our Earth Observation Data Infrastructure (EODI) automates how satellite and drone imagery is captured, processed, and delivered across commercial and sovereign constellations, allowing organisations to move from satellite tasking to actionable intelligence in minutes. We work with commercial, defence, intelligence and government customers globally, delivering highly technical data engineering and geospatial products across multi-tenant SaaS, private cloud, and on‑prem environments. This is critical infrastructure, not a dashboard toy.The challenge: You’ll be working with complex geospatial systems, strict security and reliability requirements, and customers who run critical operations on top of our platform.You’ll take ownership of a product suite operating under real constraints and tight timelines where your actions will have an outsized impact. If you want to solve technically hard problems and build systems that genuinely matter, this is your arena.The OpportunityWe’re hiring a Product Manager to work directly with the CEO and take ownership of execution across our product suite. You will sit at the intersection of customers, engineering, and operations, turning complex, real‑world requirements into deployed, operational software. You’ll help shape the long‑term product vision, but your primary job is to turn the roadmap into reality, shipping features that work in demanding customer environments.You will partner closely with the CEO on direction and priorities, then own requirement gathering and product of Arlula’s platform across defence, government, and commercial contexts. If you want genuine ownership, high autonomy, and a clear pathway to product leadership in a deeply technical domain, this is the role.What You’ll DeliverFirst 6 monthsOwn and execute Arlula’s product roadmap in partnership with the CEO, with a focus on getting features into production environments.Translate customer, defence, and operational requirements into clear product specs that engineering can build from.Drive delivery of complex data engineering and geospatial products from concept through deployment across SaaS, private cloud, and on‑prem.Support configuration and rollout of the platform into technical customer environments, working closely with operations.6-24 monthsStandardise product delivery and workflows so the platform can scale repeatedly across multiple customers and regions.Ensure the product suite meets the certification, assurance, and compliance bar required for defence and government use cases.Continuously lift product robustness, usability, and operational performance.Help evolve Arlula’s product vision and structure, growing into a full product leadership role as the team expands. What You BringEssential3+ years in product management or closely related roles.Strong self‑starter mindset: you take ownership, move fast, and don’t wait to be told what to do.Experience with complex, technical products (APIs, data platforms, infrastructure, or geospatial systems).Experience building product with cloud infrastructure technologies,Comfortable working closely with engineers and discussing technical trade‑offs.Track record of delivering products into real operational environments, not just prototypes or proofs of concept.Clear communicator who can work directly with customers, engineers, and executives.Demonstrated leadership potential, even if you haven’t formally managed people yet.Highly regardedExperience in rapid prototyping with AI tools.Experience with geospatial data, satellite imagery, or Earth observation workflows.Exposure to defence, government, or other regulated or secure environments.Familiarity with on‑prem deployments, and / or secure system architectures.Why Join Us?Shape how critical space‑to‑ground infrastructure is built, deployed, and scaled globally.Work directly with the CEO to make fast decisions, have direct impact, and genuine ownership of outcomes.Grow into senior product leadership as Arlula and the product organisation scale.Operate in a space where the technical bar is high and the work actually matters to national security, climate resilience, and commercial operations.If this sounds like the kind of challenge you’ve been looking for, we’d love to hear from you.Role type: Full time.Location: Sydney/Hybrid

title: People Operations Generalist & Business Partner
jd_text: Sift’s People Operations team is looking for a People Operations Generalist and Business Partner to join our team. We’re looking for someone who is passionate about all things People Ops, and who operates with uncompromising integrity, attention to detail, a can-do attitude, and a firm growth mindset. We are empowered to look beyond simple answers to complex problems instead of being bound by inflexible rules and processes, and building for capability instead of just compliance. We constantly aspire to do more with less, be more agile, more innovative, and to push the boundaries of what is expected.About the team:At Sift, our mission is simple yet powerful: Help everyone trust the internet. Unsurprisingly, the People Operations team believes that people are critical to the success of our mission. Every day, we work to help grow and enable our employees; maintaining a culture that is true to our values and aspirations is what motivates our team every day.We’re looking for a People Operations professional who blends strategic HR business partnering with hands‑on HR generalist and operations work. As part of a small, high‑performing team, you’ll move fluidly between advising leaders on people strategy and rolling up your sleeves to build, improve, and run the day‑to‑day programs that make Sift a great place to work.You’ll support selected business groups as a trusted advisor, while also owning and evolving the people programs, processes, and systems that power our employee experience. This is an ideal role for someone who is energized by both big‑picture thinking and operational excellence, and who enjoys building structure in a dynamic environment.Role ResponsibilitiesEmployee Experience (40%)Be an advocate and resource for employees, serving as a first point of contact for questions and day‑to‑day support. Provide clear, practical guidance on People Ops guidelines, processes, and programs.Partner with the broader Employee Experience team to design and drive initiatives that strengthen engagement, culture, and recognition.Help build, facilitate, and iterate on employee programs such as orientation, management training, and People Ops initiatives, as well as diversity, equity & inclusion work.Ensure a cohesive, values‑aligned employee experience across the organization by connecting feedback from the business back into our programs and processes.Business Partnership (40%)Serve as the primary People Business Partner for assigned functions, building strong relationships with leaders and managers as a thought partner on people strategy, org design, and change management.Coach leaders and managers on feedback, development, performance, and difficult conversations, helping them build inclusive, high‑performing teams.Provide guidance and support during key people programs (growth cycles, talent reviews, compensation and promotion cycles, engagement surveys), ensuring consistency, fairness, and manager readiness.Use data, insights, and listening to diagnose issues and co‑create solutions with your client groups, balancing business needs with a great employee experience.Operations & Compliance (20%)Create, maintain, and improve People Ops templates, documentation, workflows, and playbooks so they are simple, clear, and easy to use.Maintain employee data, systems, documentation, and processes with a focus on accuracy, scalability, and usability.Act as a point of contact for employee immigration processes and collaborate closely with Sift’s immigration counsel.Proactively identify opportunities to simplify, automate, or clarify People processes, contributing to our goal of “working smarter, not harder.” Continuously apply innovations such as advanced workflows, automation, and AI utilization.Support periodic and ongoing compliance tracking and reporting; maintain a strong working knowledge of relevant employment laws to help reduce risk and ensure compliance.Partner with third‑party vendors to administer benefits, leaves of absence, and PEO employee workflows.Desired Skills And Attributes5+ years of experience desired in HR/People Operations roles. Prior experience at a technology company strongly preferred.Experience working with ADP Workforce Now and other HRIS systems strongly preferred.Proficiency with Google Workspace and spreadsheets is required.Solid knowledge of HR Practices, Federal & State employment laws.Strong project management skills, strong attention to detail, strong work ethic, and written/oral communication skills. Excellent collaboration and teamwork skills.Self-driven, with a proven ability to work independently and exercise good judgment in fast-paced, dynamic environments.Unquestionable integrity and ability to handle confidential information professionally and with discretion.PHR/SPHR certification (or similar) a plus.A little about us:Sift is the AI-powered fraud platform securing digital trust for leading global businesses. Our deep investments in machine learning and user identity, a data network scoring 1 trillion events per year, and a commitment to long-term customer success empower more than 700 customers to grow fearlessly. Global brands rely on Sift to unlock growth and deliver seamless consumer experiences. Visit us at sift.com and follow us on LinkedIn.Benefits and Perks:Competitive total compensation package401k planMedical, dental and vision coverageWellness reimbursementEducation reimbursementFlexible time offLet’s build it together:At Sift, we are intentionally building a diverse, equitable, and inclusive workplace. We believe that diversity drives innovation, equity is a fundamental right, and inclusion is a basic human need. We envision a place where all Sifties feel secure sharing their authentic selves and diverse experiences with their teams, their customers, and their community – ultimately using this empowerment and authenticity to build trust and create a safer Internet.This document provides transparency around how Sift handles the personal data of job applicants: https://sift.com/recruitment-privacy

title: Principal Software Engineer - Fintech Global Leader
jd_text: Principal Software Engineer – Global FintechAbout The RoleWe are seeking an accomplished Principal Software Engineer to lead the design and development of mission-critical financial platforms. In this role, you will architect scalable, secure, and high-performance systems that power global payments, trading, and digital banking services. You will provide technical direction, influence strategy, and mentor teams to deliver innovative solutions in one of the most dynamic and regulated industries in the world.ResponsibilitiesDefine and drive the architecture for complex, distributed fintech platforms.Lead end-to-end design and implementation of core services supporting millions of users.Ensure regulatory, compliance, and security requirements are embedded into technical solutions.Mentor senior engineers, providing guidance on technical design, coding practices, and scalability patterns.Collaborate with product and business leaders to align technical investments with market opportunities.Champion engineering excellence through code reviews, testing strategies, and continuous improvement.Qualifications10+ years of professional software engineering experience, with deep expertise in Java, C++, or Python.Proven track record designing and scaling distributed systems in financial services or other highly regulated industries.Expert knowledge of microservices, cloud-native architectures, and event-driven design.Strong understanding of database systems, both relational and NoSQL, and data modeling at scale.Ability to influence cross-functional stakeholders and align engineering with business strategy.Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field.About AndiamoTalent Partners for the AI Revolution. As a globally recognized staffing and consulting firm, we specialize in placing the top 2% of technology and go-to-market professionals with the world’s largest and most well-known companies.For over 20 years, we've maintained the status of tier-one vendor for firms such as Palantir, Amazon, Fluidstack, Bloomberg, Relativity Space, Firefly, MasterCard, Visa, Two Sigma, Citadel, as well as other major financial services firms, elite hedge funds, Google-backed tech start-ups, and major software firms.Our talent solutions include Permanent Placement, Contract Staffing, Executive Search, and Dedicated Recruiting Services (RPO). Find out more at www.andiamogo.com

title: Director Of Engineering
jd_text: Director of Engineering w/ National Leader in Electronics ManufacturingOur client, a leading Electronics Manufacturing Services (EMS) provider, is seeking a Director of Engineering to join their high‑performance team in Newark, NY. This is a high‑impact leadership role overseeing Manufacturing Engineering, Process Engineering, and Test Engineering while supporting top customers across Aerospace, Defense, Medical, and other high‑reliability sectors.If you're a seasoned engineering leader passionate about operational excellence, team development, and driving innovation—this role offers major visibility, organizational influence, and long‑term growth.Benefits:$170,000 – $200,000 base salaryAnnual bonusFull benefits: Medical, Dental, Vision, Life, Disability401(k) with matchLeadership visibility + influence across corporate and site engineeringWork with cutting‑edge, mission‑critical technology and customersQualifications:Bachelor’s in Engineering (electronics/manufacturing focus ideal)10+ years in electronics manufacturing, including 5+ in leadershipEMS experience preferred; Aerospace/Defense/Medical experience highly valuedStrong technical depth in at least one engineering disciplineProven ability to develop teams and elevate engineering operationsExperience managing complex projects and strategic initiativesExcellent communication with both executives and customersMust be a U.S. citizen, permanent resident, or otherwise eligible under INAAppy Today or contact Cassidy Singer at cassidysinger@srstaffing.com as our client is scheduling interviews now!!!

title: Engineering Intern, CloudKitchens - Seattle
jd_text: Who We AreAt CloudKitchens, we're building the infrastructure to make food delivery as inexpensive as cooking your own meals (hear about it from our CEO, the founder of Uber here). We are working on robotics, AI, real estate intelligence, and delivery networks to build the future of food.This market is projected to be worth a trillion dollars by 2030, and we are an early market leader with a significant technical edge. You will be solving problems no one has cracked, at scale, and given autonomy to innovate.Check our tech blog to find out more about what we’re building.Check out some of our products: CloudKitchens | Otter | Picnic | Lab37Who You Are:Actively pursuing a Bachelor's/Master's/PhD in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical fieldGraduating in Fall 2026, Spring/Summer 2027.Must have completed an introductory Computer Science courseHave advanced programming experience in one or more application or systems languages (Go, Python, Ruby, Java, Kotlin, C/C++, etc.)A problem-solver who thrives in tackling diverse technical challenges and demonstrates a clear track record of growth and learningA take-charge individual who exhibits ownership and takes pride in writing clean, maintainable, and fun codeAn engineer who can dive deep into open source software, find issues, and fix them.What You’ll DoAs an engineering intern at CloudKitchens, you'll be an integral part of building out the state-of-the-art intelligent machine for the food industry. Your role will encompass a diverse range of responsibilities, including:You will build foundational software systems to enable globally scaled kitchen infrastructure and real estate innovationsYou test software development methodology in an agile environmentYou provide ongoing maintenance, support, and enhancements in existing systems and platformsYou work alongside other engineers to elevate technology and improve systems overallYou write, debug, maintain, and test codeYou design scalable, fault-tolerant architectural systemsYou develop information systems by designing, developing, and installing software solutionsYou drive innovation within the team and support CloudKitchens’ technological advancements Why join us Growing market: You’ll be focused on an $80 billion market that’s projected to reach at least $500 billion by 2030 in the US alone. Changing the restaurant industry: You’ll be part of a team that helps restaurants succeed in online food delivery. Collaborative environment: You will receive support and guidance from experienced colleagues and managers, helping you to learn, grow, and achieve your goals, and you’ll work closely with other teams to ensure our customers’ success.World-class talent - you will work with a class of highly skilled, motivated, and experienced engineers with whom you can learn and grow.How To ApplySubmit your resume to the job site. Please make sure you have your LinkedIn profile and GitHub profile included in the resume. Also include your GPA and grades for relevant courses. Thanks!What Else You Need To KnowThis role is based in our Seattle, WA office. We believe that people do their best work when they are together. As a company, we’re in the marketplace of ideas and innovation. When you’re constantly innovating, changing how an industry works, inventing new products and processes - and we are doing all these things - we believe we’re better as a team in person. That’s why all of our teams (except for our field-based roles) are now working from one of our office locations 5 days a week. Looking forward to sharing more about a Career of Substance at CloudKitchens!Job location - in-person, Seattle, WA, USACompensation - $70 per hour.
